text:
"The school of dilution and adaptation 

Question:
What do you mean by the dilution (tamyee) and adaptation (tatwee) that you mentioned?

Shaykh Abu Yahya al-Libi (may Allah have mercy upon him) said:

This approach is one of the afflictions that has befallen our Islamic Ummah in this age, and many have been tested by it, those described as thinkers, enlightened individuals, moderates, or centrists.

The reality of this methodology is the dilution of the truths of the deen and their reshaping and subjugation so that they conform to understandings, perspectives, and convictions that its proponents have adopted, especially in matters where they feel there is conflict or tension with ideas promoted by Westerners.

So you find these adapters sparing no effort and leaving no means unused to try to prove that Islam agrees with Western ideas, matches their rulings, or even preceded them in what they have proposed.

Unfortunately, this contagion has affected many Muslim scholars and preachers, to the point that some of them have become leaders of this school, those scholars upon whom Allah placed the responsibility of conveying His message to people as it is, without distortion or deception, and who should not be swayed by desire or fear.

As Allah the Exalted said:

Those who convey the messages of Allah and fear Him, and do not fear anyone except Allah. And sufficient is Allah as a Reckoner.

This school of dilution and adaptation has made all matters of the deen into an open domain, freely entered by their intellects, polluted by their ideas, and subjected to their research, without any restraint or trustworthiness holding them back.

This is a great calamity of our time.

The Quran has already clarified this reality and exposed the inner motives of such people, namely deviation and corruption of the heart, which can be summarized as infatuation with Western culture, and being carried away by intellectual temptation.

Allah says:

As for those in whose hearts is deviation, they follow what is ambiguous, seeking Fitna and seeking its interpretation.

The Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) recited this verse and then said:

If you see those who follow what is ambiguous, they are the ones Allah has named, so beware of them.

Therefore, a Muslim, so that his deen remains sound and he protects himself from deviation and getting lost in paths of misguidance, must beware of such people and not be deceived by names, titles, or fame.

What they are doing is polluting our Islamic sources and corrupting our pure concepts, which Islam was keen to preserve in their independence, in terminology, meanings, and substance, so that no impurity would enter and disturb this pure spring.

In this regard, there is the incident when the Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) was approached by Umar (may Allah be pleased with him), who said:
We hear statements from the Jews that we find appealing, should we write some of them down?

The Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam) replied:

Are you becoming confused as the Jews and Christians became confused? I have brought it to you pure and clear. If Musa were alive, he would have no choice but to follow me.

Today, you see that whenever a Western thinker praises something from Islam, it is taken as proof of the truth of the message, as though we are in need of such endorsements.
Even worse, and more dangerous, is that the situation has reversed, these people of such thinking now reshape Islamic meanings so that they serve as evidence for the correctness of many aspects of Western culture, even when that culture fundamentally contradicts Islam in both concepts and terminology.

I believe we are in need of a thorough study of the foundations and principles of this deviant and manipulative school, in order to uproot it and expose its danger to the core principles of the religion, the extent to which it corrupts its concepts, ......

Continued  "

"Shaykh Al Islam, the Imam of the Imams, Ibn Khuzaymah Al-Shfii Al-Wahhbiyy (d. 311 AH) said about the quoted Mushrik fellow and his Jahmi cult:

Whoever does not affirm that Allah the Mighty and Majestic has Rose above His Throne above His seven heavens  he is a disbeliever in his Lord, his blood is lawful. He is to be called to repent; if he repents, well and good, otherwise his neck is to be struck, and he is to be thrown onto some of the rubbish heaps so that neither the Muslims nor the dhimms are harmed by the putrid stench of his corpse. And his wealth becomes fay  no Muslim inherits from him, for a Muslim does not inherit from a disbeliever, as the Prophet  said: A Muslim does not inherit from a disbeliever, nor a disbeliever from a Muslim."

text:
"Allmah Ibn Taymiyyah was asked about saying L Ilha ill Allh 70,000 times and gifting it to the dead. He responded: If he does it like this 70,000 times, or more, or less, and gifts it Allh will benefit them with it. And this has no [basis] in neither authentic nor weak Hadth.

 [Majm al-Fatw, 24/323, translation from @ahnaafservices Twitter]

Benefits:

1. Ihda As-Sawab (Urdu Isaal e Sawab), or gifting deeds to the dead, is something that was generally considered permissible.

2. It's not necessary that every particular deed related to Sadaqah Jariyah is proven from the Sahabah. For ex. It's not necessary to show that the Sahabah donated an X-ray machine to a hospital with the intention of Sadaqah Jariyah.

3. A precedent in the actions of the Sahabah. - Summary- Unable to financially contribute to the Tabuk expedition, Ulbah bin Zayd (Radi Allahu Anhu) wept in prayer and sincerely offered his personal honor as charity by forgiving any Muslim who had wronged him. The next morning, the Prophet (Salallahu Alaihi Wa Sallam) informed him that Allah had recognized his pure intention and officially accepted his act of forgiveness as a highly rewarded charity. (Seerat Ibn Hisham (Vol. 2, p. 516) and Zaad al-Ma'ad by Ibn al-Qayyim (Vol. 3, p. 461), authentic.)

4. This also means, reciting the Quran and gifting it to the dead is permissible. Ibn Taymiyyah held that opinion. In our contemporary Ulama, Shaykh Muhammad bin Saleh Al-Uthaymeen said that it was permissible to gift deeds to the dead."

text:
"Regarding Pakistan / Afghanistan relations 

From my friend @LongshotIbrahim -- a brother who has written on and studied the subject considerably:

I have mentioned this before but in 1980s/1990s various Afghan factions, incl both Taliban and some of their foes, supported Pakistan and Kashmir and went out of the way to do so. This was while an increasingly pro-India US was running interference for India.

While Afg mujahideen in general backed Pakistan and Kashmir, outstanding case was Hikmatyar (Afg prime minister in 1993-94, then 1995-96), and his field commander Khalid Faruqi. Hizbul Mujahideen were trained and at least partly modelled on them for a decade or so.

Another major case was Arsala-Rahmani Daulat (prime minister 1994-95), who later joined Taliban and was murdered in 2012. A third was justice minister Jalaluddin Haqqani, father of today's Taliban interior minister Sirajuddin.

In general Taliban remained pro-Pakistan thru their first emirate. During the 1999 Pak war w India over Kashmir, Taliban prime minister Mohammad Rabbani offered to send thousands of fighters (but was declined bcos there wasnt really a manpower shortage).

At the end of 1999, Pak militants related to Kashmir jihad incl. Masood Azhar were released from Indian custody in a hostage exchange mediated, much to Pakistani glee, by Taliban Kandahar corps commander Akhtar Usmani.

Many of Kashmir-focused mujahideen also supported Taliban in late 1990s incl Saifullah Akhtar and AbdulJabbar Farooq. They fought directly under defence minister Ubaidullah Akhund and his lieutenant, today's Afghan security minister Ibrahim Sadar.

In east Afg, Taliban and Hizb and also Salafi networks were linked to Kashmir-focused mujahideen incl Salafi "Lashkar Taiba". One commander in Kunar who latee fought US at the famed Korengal valley, AbdulRahim, was a Hizbi turned Taliban but of Salafi persuasion, close to Taiba.

In 2001-02 after US invasion, Pakistan banned some of these groups (but tacitly tolerated others like Taiba). AbdulJabbar and Saifullah as well as Ilyas Kashmiri revolted against Pakistan, while Khalil and Azhar nervously walked a fine line.

In 2004, partly bcos of these rebels but also largely to distract US after Iran/Libya tattled, Pakistani army invaded Waziristan region. The army was surprised at local tribal backlash and adopted the line "Why are yoy fighting us, go and fight US in Afghanistan"

This line was supported by Afghan Taliban and practically every one of their leaders - emir Umar, Jalaluddin and Sirajuddin, Usmani, Daddullah Lang, Ubaidullah, chief justice NurMohammad Saqib, Mohammad Yasir, Sadar Azam - mediated on Pakistan's behalf with Waziristan insurgency.

However US intelligence (CIA) infiltrated Pakistan in 2005, using earthquake relief and humanitarian aid as a cover. Their targets were not only Taliban or Qaeda but also Pakistani groups linked to Kashmir, such as Taiba

Since the late 1990s US stance on Kashmir had been "Pakistan should forget Kashmir, make peace with India, and focus on its own Islamic radicalism", and this is what US (and their local lapdogs in Pakistan) pursued.

US also repeatedly sabotaged deals and ceasefires that, mediated by Taliban, army had made with insurgency in Waziristan. Ie it was US, not Taliban, who put Pakistan army and civilians at risk; it was US, not Taliban, who undermined Kashmir issue

Despite this Pakistan scrambled to appease and placate US, who would constantly bark "Do more" and undermine Pakistan further. It wasnt until 2011 that CIA agents were expelled as US/Pak relations hit a low and by then US was cracking down on Kashmir activists too

Meanwhile despite the Pak/US relations various Taliban networks incl Sirajuddin Haqqani continued to hit India; e.g. in 2008 Indian attache Ravi Mehta was killed at Kabul in a joint op w/ Taiba.

Sirajuddin was particularly hated by US for his role in a "triple agent" trick that killed CIA field commander Jennifer Matthews in what is still regarded as one of the most humiliating episodes in CIA history.

One CIA agent from this period - when agency was systematically destabilizing Pakistan, vilifying Kashmir, and bolstering India - was Sarah Adams, who has a particular visceral hatred for Sirajuddin Haqqani even by the agency's standards

Now bear in mind Adams' background. She was working for CIA in region at a time when it was actively involved in what even the most charitable appraisal could call anti-Pakistan/anti-Kashmir activity, during a period when Taliban were doing what Pakistan requested incl. v India.

however, after 2022 Pak coup - which was at least partly done for US approval - Pakistani pseudo-nationalists have embraced Adams as an unimpeachable source whenever she blames Taliban for anything, bcos they are eager to offload any responsibility onto "those ingrate Afghans"

Adams blames Taliban for everything, and she does this in large part bcos of her background and her resentment at Haqqanis, in particular. In 2023 she claimed that Sirajuddin had masterminded Hamas/Hezbollah war v Israel (oh, of course she also hates Palestine & loves Israel)

Adams doesn't give a shit abt Kashmir. She is pro-Israel, and Israel is pro-India, and India is anti-Kashmir. Her CIA career was spent partly in pro-India/anti-Kashmir activity. However, she knows that Pakistanis care about Kashmir and she wants to turn Pakistanis against Taliban

so after pro-US 2022 coup in Pakistan, Adams gets onto podcasts and "exposes" her target: "Oh, Taliban betrayed Kashmiris; oh, Taliban backstabbed Pakistan". If she could, she'd accuse Taliban of murdering Liaquat Ali-Khan in 1951 in order to whip up anti-Afghan sentiment in Pak

The fact is Taliban have a long record of supporting Pakistan and even Kashmir, and a looong record of opposing India at least on this issue. Whereas US,and its agents like Adams, have exactly the opposite record. But to justify current Pak war v Afg, she spreads these daft tales

Also, Taliban government is more or less the same ppl, with a handful of younger faces, as it was in 1996-2001 when they supported Pakistan. None of these people were anti-Pakistan until Pakistan, after the 2022 coup, started bombarding them."

The army was surprised at local tribal backlash and adopted the line "Why are yoy fighting us, go and fight US in Afghanistan" This line was supported by Afghan Taliban and practically every one of their leaders - emir Umar, Jalaluddin and Sirajuddin, Usmani, Daddullah Lang, Ubaidullah, chief justice NurMohammad Saqib, Mohammad Yasir, Sadar Azam - mediated on Pakistan's behalf with Waziristan insurgency. However US intelligence (CIA) infiltrated Pakistan in 2005, using earthquake relief and humanitarian aid as a cover. Their targets were not only Taliban or Qaeda but also Pakistani groups linked to Kashmir, such as Taiba Since the late 1990s US stance on Kashmir had been "Pakistan should forget Kashmir, make peace with India, and focus on its own Islamic radicalism", and this is what US (and their local lapdogs in Pakistan) pursued. US also repeatedly sabotaged deals and ceasefires that, mediated by Taliban, army had made with insurgency in Waziristan. Ie it was US, not Taliban, who put Pakistan army and civilians at risk; it was US, not Taliban, who undermined Kashmir issue Despite this Pakistan scrambled to appease and placate US, who would constantly bark "Do more" and undermine Pakistan further. It wasnt until 2011 that CIA agents were expelled as US/Pak relations hit a low and by then US was cracking down on Kashmir activists too Meanwhile despite the Pak/US relations various Taliban networks incl Sirajuddin Haqqani continued to hit India; e.g. in 2008 Indian attache Ravi Mehta was killed at Kabul in a joint op w/ Taiba. Sirajuddin was particularly hated by US for his role in a "triple agent" trick that killed CIA field commander Jennifer Matthews in what is still regarded as one of the most humiliating episodes in CIA history. One CIA agent from this period - when agency was systematically destabilizing Pakistan, vilifying Kashmir, and bolstering India - was Sarah Adams, who has a particular visceral hatred for Sirajuddin Haqqani even by the agency's standards Now bear in mind Adams' background. She was working for CIA in region at a time when it was actively involved in what even the most charitable appraisal could call anti-Pakistan/anti-Kashmir activity, during a period when Taliban were doing what Pakistan requested incl. v India. however, after 2022 Pak coup - which was at least partly done for US approval - Pakistani pseudo-nationalists have embraced Adams as an unimpeachable source whenever she blames Taliban for anything, bcos they are eager to offload any responsibility onto "those ingrate Afghans" Adams blames Taliban for everything, and she does this in large part bcos of her background and her resentment at Haqqanis, in particular. In 2023 she claimed that Sirajuddin had masterminded Hamas/Hezbollah war v Israel (oh, of course she also hates Palestine & loves Israel) Adams doesn't give a shit abt Kashmir. She is pro-Israel, and Israel is pro-India, and India is anti-Kashmir. Her CIA career was spent partly in pro-India/anti-Kashmir activity. However, she knows that Pakistanis care about Kashmir and she wants to turn Pakistanis against Taliban so after pro-US 2022 coup in Pakistan, Adams gets onto podcasts and "exposes" her target: "Oh, Taliban betrayed Kashmiris; oh, Taliban backstabbed Pakistan". If she could, she'd accuse Taliban of murdering Liaquat Ali-Khan in 1951 in order to whip up anti-Afghan sentiment in Pak The fact is Taliban have a long record of supporting Pakistan and even Kashmir, and a looong record of opposing India at least on this issue. Whereas US,and its agents like Adams, have exactly the opposite record. But to justify current Pak war v Afg, she spreads these daft tales Also, Taliban government is more or less the same ppl, with a handful of younger faces, as it was in 1996-2001 when they supported Pakistan. None of these people were anti-Pakistan until Pakistan, after the 2022 coup, started bombarding them."

text:
"So we rejected this statement and said: this is a dangerous statement. Its implication is that you are nullifying the Fatw of the scholars in Nawzil. If you see that there is a mistake, and you are a Salaf who respects the scholars and also respects the rulers, then write to them. Present your viewpoint only. But to establish this as a general principlethis is false and wrong! 

This resembles the approach of the Surrs and Qubs during the Gulf crisis, who said: Our scholars do not understand current affairswe understand current affairs. America entered, Europe did such-and-such, and you, O scholarslike ibn Bzyou are only with the rulers, obeying them. The same wordsonly the people and circumstances have changed. There it was a Gulf crisis related to the entry of disbelievers (to the lands of the Muslims); here it is a COVID crisis related to medicine.

Furthermore, this man (making these claims) works in medicine. If we look at his specialization, do you work in every field of medicine and every specialty? No. Then speak only within your level. That is one point.

Secondly, do you want the entire Ummah to abandon the doctors of the world and the consultants, and instead follow your opinion? This is dangerous speech! It is unclear what this person intends with such statements. 

Despite that, he has been advised, and we have advised him before. We even sent to him years ago when he issued a Fatw. Look at this Fatwmany people may not even be aware of it. He issued a ruling that social distancing was Minor Shirk. That means Al-Fawzn has fallen into Shirk, the Muft has fallen into Shirk, and our scholars such as Ubayd and others have fallen into Shirk. And we, the students of knowledge, have all fallen into Shirk. And the Fatw of the scholars who permitted distancing are, according to him, Fatw of Shirk.

So who is behind you that you speak with such words? What do you want, that you strike at the scholars by claiming they have fallen into (minor) Shirk, that they should not be referred to, and that they neither understand nor comprehend? This is what he says in the audio recordings. 

Some have not even listened to these recordings yet and do not want to listenand this is a calamity. You find a person plunging headlong into Fitnah while knowing nothing. And if you say to him, Come, let us sit and clarify things for you, he refuses to listen. This is blind partisanship. 

As for us, we clarify the error. Whoever is guided, then all praise is due to Allh, and whoever goes astray, then it is upon himself.

Shaykh Abdul-Azz ibn Bz was asked on his official site: Should we refer only to doctors, or to the scholars? He replied: No. One must return to the scholar. If the matter were purely medical, then yeswe would return to the doctors; because they are the people of specialization. But this concerns a matter connected to medicine and also to the Sharah.

Does Al-Fawzn immediately issue a ruling when a purely medical issue comes to him? You think highly of yourself and consider yourself an expert. But when this scholarAl-Fawzn, the Muft, Al-Abbd, Rab, and othersare asked about a worldly technical matter such as medicine or agriculture, do they refer to specialists or not? They do refer to the specialists. This shows that you do not understand how the scholars issue Fatw.

I have already explained this in detail in one of the refutationsthe methodology of the scholar in giving Fatwand it is published on my channel. Yet some people ignore all of this and do not want to listen. 

Continued"

text:
""How the Salaf would read Quran

Shaykh Abdussalam as-Shuwayer   
"
--------------

How Often Should I Read the Qurn?
 
Shaykh Muammad bin Al bin dam al-Ityb   said:

 this shows that, at the very least, reading the [entire] Qurn should be done monthly; every night, you should read one juz until you complete it at the end of the month. This is the longest period of time it should be. Then, if you have desire, in twenty, fifteen, and so on, and do not do it more than every seven days; seven days was the standard of the Salaf: the majority of the Salaf used to read the Qurn in seven parts; they split it into seven portions, and so they would finish it every week.

Tr: Ab Hurayrah al-Birn

"The Prophet  said:      (There is no marriage without the permission of the wal). Sunan Ab Dwd 2805, h (al-Albn).

Ikhwanis: Parent/guardian can be present in a marriage sit-down, but they are requested not to interfere with the sessions.

So the guardian cannot "interfere", and as to add to the humiliation, he should pay a fee to "observe", whilst his daughter is in a "rotational setting" meeting other men. He is the wal, he should be jealous over his ward and not exhibit beta-male energy.

For the people who lazily proclaim: Why do you Salafis busy yourself with refutations?

This is why. We observe nonsense like this daily and out of love for Kitb and Sunnah and love for the truth to be upheld, we speak out. This is the vigilance every Muslim should have in defending the Sunnah and uniting the people upon it.

Porting speed dating concepts into Islamic marriage meet-ups is humiliation and ignorance, adopting the methods of the disbelievers. It reminds us of the narration:

Ab Hurayrah (Allah be pleased with him) narrated that the Prophet () said: The Hour will not be established until my Ummah will take the ways of those generations (nations) that preceded them, following them handspan by handspan, cubit-length by cubit-length. They asked: O Allahs Messengers! Such as the Persians and Romans? He replied: Who else except for them. (al-Bukhr, no. 7319)

We ask Allh for safety.",

text:
"Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar    was among the most devoted, scrupulous, and steadfast Companions of the Messenger of Allh . He was a man whose life became a living embodiment of the Sunnah, so precise in his adherence that he would follow even the smallest actions of the Prophet  out of love and reverence.

He was Abdullh ibn Umar ibn al-Khab al-Adaw al-Qurash, the son of the great Caliph Sayyidun Umar ibn al-Khab   . He was born in Makkah before the Hijrah and embraced Islm at a young age alongside his noble father. He emigrated to al-Madnah al-Munawwarah at the age of ten, and thus was counted among the early Muhjirn, those whom Allh, Most High, praised in His Book:

And the foremost, the first of the Muhjirn and the Anr and those who followed them with excellence, Allh is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him, and He has prepared for them gardens beneath which rivers flow, wherein they will abide forever. That is the great success.

[Qurn 9:100]

In his youth, he longed to participate in battle alongside the Messenger of Allh . He was presented at Badr but was turned back due to his young age. He was again presented at Uud, when he was only thirteen years old, hoping to join the ranks, but he was sent back by the Prophet  due to his youth, alongside Sayyidun Usmah ibn Zayd   . That moment weighed heavily upon him, and he later said: 

A night like that never came my way, filled with sleeplessness, sadness, and weeping, because the Messenger of Allh  did not accept me.

After returning him from Uud, the Messenger of Allh  appointed him among those tasked with guarding the children in Madnah. 

Yet his heart remained attached to striving in the path of Allh, and his sincere supplication to be permitted to fight was soon answered. In the fifth year after Hijrah, when he reached the age of fifteen, he was allowed to participate in the Battle of al-Khandaq. 

From that point forward, he took part in the major campaigns of Islm. He participated in the campaign against Ban Qurayah, the journey to al-udaybiyyah, and was among those who pledged beneath the tree in the Pledge of al-Riwn, a moment honored by Allh, Most High:

Indeed, Allh was pleased with the believers when they pledged allegiance to you under the tree. He knew what was in their hearts, so He sent down tranquility upon them and rewarded them with a near victory.

[Qurn 48:18]

He later joined the campaigns of Khaybar, Mutah, the Conquest of Makkah, the expedition against Ban Judhaymah, the Battle of unayn, and the march to Tabk. He was also among the soldiers of the army of Sayyidun Usmah ibn Zayd   . 

After returning from this expedition, he volunteered in the wars against apostasy and had the honor of participating in the Battle of Yammah against Musaylamah al-Kadhdhb. 

He later marched to Shm and fought at al-Yarmk, then joined the campaigns in the Persian lands under Sayyidun Sad ibn Ab Waqq   , attending al-Qdisiyyah, Jall, and the conquest of al-Madin. He also took part in the conquest of Egypt and campaigns in North Africa. 

Even in his later years, he joined the expedition toward Constantinople, about which the Prophet  said: The first army to attack the city of Caesar is forgiven.

What distinguished Ibn Umar    above all was his extraordinary commitment to following the Sunnah. He would carefully observe where the Prophet  prayed, where he sat, and even the paths he walked, then imitate them with remarkable precision. 

If he saw the Prophet  perform prayer in a particular place, he would later pray in that very same place. If he saw him make supplication while standing, he would supplicate while standing; and if he saw him invoke Allh while sitting, he would do the same. His life was not merely one of narration, but of complete imitation.

He was among the foremost scholars of the Companions and a prolific narrator of hadith, transmitting over 2,600 narrations. His knowledge was paired with deep caution, he was known for his reluctance to issue legal verdicts without certainty, and he preferred silence over speculation. 

Due to his age, knowledge, and unwavering adherence to the Sunnah, he became known in his time as the Shaykh of the abah, and even powerful governors such as al-ajjj ibn Ysuf treated him with respect and deference due to his immense status amongst the Muslims. From the greatest testimonies to his precision in transmitting the Sunnah is that he stands at the head of one of the most authentic chains in hadith, the Golden Chain.

Mlik  Nfi  Ibn Umar.

A generation of leading Tbin took knowledge from him, including Sad ibn al-Musayyib, Thbit al-Bunn, ws, al-Zuhr, Muammad ibn Srn, Nfi (his freed servant), Mujhid, and many others. His influence thus extended deeply into the next generation of scholarship. 

His legacy was also carried forward through his own household, as several of his sons became scholars, most notably Slim ibn Abdullh ibn Umar, who was counted among the great jurists of Madnah. Through both his students and his family, his knowledge and methodology continued to shape the transmission of the Sunnah for generations.

During the periods of civil strife, having lived to witness both the First and Second Fitnah among the Muslims, Ibn Umar    consistently adopted a principled stance of restraint. He presented himself at the sittings of arbitration, demonstrating his desire for unity among the Muslims. 

He famously said: When you call me to prayer, I respond. However, when you call me to fight a Muslim, I do not. He never raised his sword against the believers. Yet, on the other hand, he answered every call to strive against the disbelievers, embodying the words of Allh, Most High:

Humble toward the believers, powerful against the disbelievers; they strive in the cause of Allh 

[Qurn 5:54]

He was known for his humility, generosity, and detachment from worldly life. Wealth would come to him, yet he would spend it freely in charity. He freed many slaves and cared deeply for the poor and needy. His nights were often spent in prayer, and his days in fasting and remembrance.

Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar    passed away in Makkah in 73 AH and was buried near Dh uw, leaving behind a legacy of unwavering adherence to the Sunnah and a life defined by sincerity and caution.

May Allh be pleased with Sayyidun Abdullh ibn Umar   , the devoted follower of the Sunnah and one of the greatest scholars among the Companions of the Messenger of Allh ."

text:
" Why One Raman Can Change Your Life!

 Sh Abu Idrees Muhammad Khn ( ).

 @AbuIdrees | @AstonMasjid 

 

  Hadith of two men who embraced Islam together; however, one would strive more than the other. He died during a military expedition. The other man lived a year more. alhah ibn Ubaydillh narrates that he saw in a dream that he was at the door of Paradise, and he saw these two men. A man from Jannah came and permitted the man who lived an extra year to enter first.
 The people were astonished that the one who died in a military expedition and strove in his Islam didnt enter Jannah first. The explanation given by the Prophet : The one who lived for an extra year reached another Raman, he fasted and prayed. The comparison between the two men in rewards is as vast as the heavens and the earth.
 The importance and virtues of Raman and the rewards that are to be gained during this month.
 The encouragement to capitalise in this month.
 The vast opportunities to gain Allhs Forgiveness in Raman.
 Let this month be for you and not against you.",

text:
"  ,     

Shaykh Muhammad ibn 'Abdullatf l Ash-Shaykh said:

"Allh - blessed and exalted be He - deals with His servants according to how they place Him as it relates to themselves.

Whoever reveres the command of Allh, obeys Him, avoids His prohibitions, and fears Him in secret and in public, Allh will be pleased with him and grant him happiness.

But whomsoever opposes His commands and embarks upon His prohibitions, and gives precedence to his desires over obedience to his Master, Allh will take retribution against him and distance him. As you act, so shall you be treated; an appropriate recompense. And your Lord is not at all unjust to (His) slaves."

 Durar as-Siniyyah fil Ajwibah an-Najdiah (14/289)"

text:
Upon the authority of Abu Hurairah, the Messenger of Allh (sallallhu 'alaihi wa sallam) said: 

When the month of Ramadan enters, the doors of Paradise are opened, the doors of Hellfire are closed, and the (chief) devils are chained.
[Bukhari, Muslim]

Shaykh Ibn Baz (May Allh have mercy on him) mentioned in explanation of the above hadith:

"This indicates the virtue of this great month and that Allah, Glorified and Exalted, grants His servants success in performing many righteous deeds during it, deeds for which the doors of Paradise are opened and the doors of the heavens are opened, as occurs in another narration: The doors of the heavens are opened.

In this is an encouragement to increase in acts of goodness such as prayer, giving charity, saying Subhnallh, saying L ilha illallh, and other than that to seize this opportunity; the opportunity of this great month and what it contains of Allahs generosity, His kindness, and the vastness of His benevolence.

Likewise, (seizing the opportunity found in) the chaining of the (chief) devils and their inability to reach what they used to reach (of evil) in other months, and that their evil is weakened."

 Explanation of Shaykh ibn Baz on Riyd As-Slihn (2nd explanation, no. 389) - taken from the Shaykh's website.

text:
""Wahhabism is the tool of extremism and modern day terrorism. 

It began as theology fused with power.

Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab was an 18th-century cleric from Najd who preached uncompromising monotheism and condemned what he saw as religious deviation. But his defining weapon was not spirituality. It was takfir: declaring other Muslims outside the fold.

And even inside his own home, there was alarm.

His father, Abd al-Wahhab ibn Sulayman, reportedly warned people not to follow his sons extremism. His own brother, Sulayman ibn Abd al-Wahhab, wrote an entire refutation:

      
Al-Sawaiq al-Ilahiyya fi al-Radd ala al-Wahhabiyya
(Divine Thunderbolts in Refutation of the Wahhabis)

In it, he warned that reckless takfir leads to bloodshed. He compared the movement to early Kharijite extremism  religion weaponized against Muslims. And, that is exactly what happened in the following years.

Then came 1744.

The pact between Ibn Abd al-Wahhab and Muhammad bin Saud in Diriyah.

The formula was simple and ruthless:

Al-Sheikh holds religion.
Al-Saud holds power.

Through that alliance, theology became state machinery.

Expansion was framed as purification. Campaigns like the attack on Karbala in 1802 are documented in multiple chronicles. Sectarian violence was justified as religious correction. Unity meant obedience. Dissent meant deviance.

That fusion : Religion as political legitimacy became the backbone of the Saudi state model.

Fast forward to the 20th century.

Oil wealth globalized the doctrine. Religious institutions expanded. Clerical authority was centralized. Universities such as Imam Muhammad ibn Saud Islamic University became exporters of a rigid interpretation of Salafism.

Then came Afghanistan.

During the Soviet war, Saudi religious rhetoric aligned with U.S. anti-communist strategy. Arab Afghans were mobilized under religious banners. The ideological infrastructure created centuries earlier proved adaptable to modern geopolitics.

From that environment emerged figures like Osama bin Laden, founder of Al-Qaeda. Later movements from Al-Qaeda to ISIS drew heavily from Takfiri Wahhabi logic: excommunication as a political weapon to justify jihad and murder. 

This does not mean Ibn Abd al-Wahhab sat in the 18th century planning 9/11.

It means that when you normalize declaring Muslims apostates, and when state power protects that logic, you build an intellectual ecosystem extremists can later radicalize further.

The 9/11 hijackers, European terror cells, sectarian militias in Iraq and Syria  they justified violence using theological frameworks built on takfir and militant purification.

That ideological pipeline matters.

Throughout modern Middle Eastern politics, religion was repeatedly used as leverage:

Against Arab nationalism.
Against Jamal Abdulnasser.
During Cold War alignments.
During regional rivalries.

Each time, theology served strategy.

And now?

When Saudi enters political conflict whether in Yemen, Qatar, or against the UAE  the instinctive tool is familiar: religious framing. Accuse. Label. Delegitimize.

Same method. New arena.

History shows the pattern:

When religion becomes state armor, power hides behind piety.
When dissent becomes heresy, control becomes sacred.
And when takfir becomes political currency, violence follows.

The earliest warning came from inside the family itself. But were silenced so Bin Saud and Alsheikh can carry out their gruesome acts of genocide against the Shia people in Alhassa and then in 1802, Saudi-Wahhabi forces attacked Karbala and killed under the justification of Takfir."
"
--------------

Zio-Emiratism is a tool of 

The reality, as i stated before, is that many of these  funded Emiratis hate Islam and Tawheed

May Allah have mercy on Shaykh Muhammad Ibn Abdulwahhab

He is free from the lies you associate with him,

text:
Fatawa will never suffice for replacing Ahkaam.

A Fatwa is not transferable or enforceable.

A Hukm applies to all until an exception is established.

Laypeople deal with Muftis and their Fatawa as sacrosanct because they don't have access to Fuqahaa' and Ahkaam.

Whenever you hear a person begin to speak about Islamic law on the basis of Fatawa, know they are a layperson who has no standing on the matter.

Whenever you hear Muftis begin to deal with Islamic law on the basis of Fatawa or the unique, independent Ijtihad of specific scholars, know that they are bereft of Ijtihad themselves.

Safety from the corruption and mistakes of scholars is found in sticking to the Mu'tamad of the four schools of Fiqh.

ONLY IF a Mu'tamad view for a matter is not found because of a lack of precedent, then scholars are turned to for guidance.

Engaging in Ijtihad/Ifta while well-established Ahkaam exist as legal precedent is irresponsible and usually involve a corrupt agenda.

text:
""Now available: Forsaking Faith: On Apostasy and the Cautionary Story of Abd Allah Al-Qasimi. 

Book overview 

Before you is a brief but urgent treatise on the evil of apostasy. It defines the term, recounts examples of those who left the faith, examines its roots, and offers guidance on protecting the heart from it. Also included is the cautionary story of Abd Allah al-Qasimi, once called the Ibn Taymiyyah of his time, who later abandoned the religion. The book also presents a number of scholarly rulings on the matter. May Allah keep us firm upon Islam."
"
--------------

Most will think they dont need to read a book on apostasy because its not relevant to them, but the story of al-Qsim is full of warnings, esp. for students of knowledge. 

A man likened to Shaykh ul-Islm who ended up being known as the father of Arab atheism.

text:
"The Danger of Free-Mixing and a Warning to Those Who Treat the Matter Lightly

Ab Hurayrah narrated that the Messenger of Allh (   ) said:

The best rows for men are the first rows, and the worst are the last rows, and the best rows for women are the last rows, and the worst are the first rows. Muslim 440.

Al-Nawaw comments:

The reason the last rows are preferred for women who are present with men is because they are farther from mixing with the men, seeing them, becoming attached in the heart due to seeing their movements or hearing their speech, and similar matters. For this reason, the first rows are considered the opposite (less preferable) for women, and Allh knows best.

If this concern applies even in the most sacred of places, where hearts are softened, desires are restrained by the fear of Allh, and one is surrounded by righteous believers, yet unnecessary mixing between men and women is still cautioned against, then what about places of lesser sanctity, where temptation is stronger, heedlessness is more common, and restrain is weaker?

Munb al-ml"

text:
"The adversaries of tawd, the promoters of moral decay, and those calling for liberation from values, ethics, and divine commands and prohibitions; such people are increasing in this age, not diminishing. They proclaim that whoever merely says l ilha ill Allh is a believer, even if he abandons the Sharah of Allah entirely!

In their view and creed, rulings pertain only to what lies in the heart, not to outward actions. And the more pretentious among them insist that l ilha ill Allh does not encompass all aspects of life. The result of this corrupt ideology has been the spread of wickedness across the earth, the suspension of jihd in the path of Allah, and the rise of shirk, biddah, and political, economic, intellectual, and social deviations among the Muslims, whereby the true meanings of the Sharah have been lost.

Thus the Murji doctrine fused with the secular mindset that seeks to sever religion from life and life from religion. Many people came to believe that worship is confined to ritual acts performed in the home and the mosque, and that religion has no place in governance or politics. They roll off their tongues the word of unbelief: Render unto Allah what is Allahs, and unto Caesar what is Caesars.

al-Allmah Sulaymn al-Alwn - Al inna nasra Allhi qarb"

"Your reliance on Dhahabi and Ibn Hajar collapses.

Both Dhahabi and Ibn Hajar mention that al-Amashs usage of  from Abu Salih has weakness.

When a scholar mentions Jarh in one place and Tadlis in another, the Jarh supersedes the Tadil. 

Dhahabi in his Al-Muhadhdhab fi-Ikhtisar al-Sunan al-Kabir (abridgement of al-Bayhaqis Sunan al-Kabir) says when mentioning a narration of al-Amash using  Abu Salih:

"And this hadith was not heard by al A'mash from Abu Salih."

[Dhahabis Al-Muhadhdhab fi-Ikhtisar al-Sunan al-Kabir 1/425 Dar al-Watan Edition]

Ibn Hajar in Al Nukat ala Sahih al-Bukhari says:

"And al-Tirmidhi transmitted it and said: Hasan. He said:
'We did not call it sahih because it is said that al A'mash practiced tadlis in it, saying: "I was told on the authority of () Abu Salih."'

I say: However, in the narration of Muslim, from Abu Usamah, from al A'mash: Abu Salih narrated to us (), so the accusation of his tadlis is removed."

[Ibn Hajar in Al Nukat ala Sahih al-Bukhari 2/107 Al-Maktabah al-Islamiyyah Edition]

So according to Ibn Hajar the accusation of tadlis was only lifted in that particular narration (a different one to the narration of this threads topic) only due to direct hearing mentioned for it elsewhere.
Otherwise the accusation of tadlis is there."

text:
The young lion , mujhid and martyr of Chechnya 

Muhammed Yutaev (Chechen:  ), also known as Magomed Yutaev, was a young Chechen fighter born in 1980 in Shalazhi, a village in Chechnya. He is remembered for his role in the First and Second Chechen Wars during his adolescence.

In early 1995, at the age of 14, Yutaev voluntarily joined the popular militia of the Chechen Republic of Ichkeria to defend against the Russian invasion that began in December 1994. His commitment to the cause led to his selection for participation in significant operations, including Shamil Basayev's raid on Budyonnovsk in June 1995, a pivotal event in the war.

On May 21, 1996, Yutaev gave an interview to a Chechen presidential broadcaster, which brought him national recognition. Later that year, in August, he was involved in the Battle of Grozny, also known as Operation Jihad, which contributed to the partial liberation of the capital and was instrumental in securing victory in the First Chechen War.

At the end of 1999, during the onset of the Second Chechen War, Yutaev was captured by Russian forces. It is believed that he died due to torture suffered in Russian prisons . He is considered a martyr by Chechen separatists and was posthumously awarded the title "Qoman Turpal" (Hero of the Nation).

Yutaev's legacy endures as a symbol of youthful bravery in the Chechen resistance. His 1996 interview remains available online, serving as a testament to his dedication and the broader struggle for Chechen independence.

text:
"This video serves as an example of the importance of staying in your lane. 

      
Whoever speaks in matters external to his field ends up producing strange positions 

In this video, this speaker has ostracised millions of Muslims who trace their creed back to the time of the pious predecessors. 
He speaks as if the Salaf creed was born with Sh. Muhammad bin Abd al-Wahhb, whilst any basic student of Aqdah is aware that scholars of this creed predate Ibn Abd al-Wahhb by circa a thousand years. Even those who critique this creed and claim that it doesnt represent the Aqdah of the Salaf acknowledge the point that it predates Ibn Abdil Wahhb, citing Ibn Taymiyyahs creed as a prime example (d. 728AH). 

Furthermore, his categorisation of acceptable creeds into Ashar, Mturd and the Tahw creed is bizarre for multiple reasons. First, he argues that these creeds are the Aqidat al-Salaf, whilst ignoring & sidelining the creed of the Imm of Ahlus Sunnah, Ahmad bin Hanbal. Was his creed not the creed of the Salaf? Second, his mentioning of the Tahw creed as the third creed adds to his blunders. Both the Ashirah and the Mturdyyah would claim that the creed of al-Tahw reflects their Aqdah, as would the Athars. Specialists in this field from each of these schools do not treat the Tahw creed as a separate school of Aqdah. 

As for his immature, ridiculous comments like you have a mind of a nine-year-old and you have some IQ in your head?  they do not deserve a response. Such statements only reflect badly upon the one who says them. 

This is just a brief response; much more could be said on this mess of a video.",

text:
"Standard Madkhali idiocy exposed by their lacking of basic reading comprehension, as well as doing us a favor by using as proof the quote (likely copied and pasted from another Madkhali Zindq) that refutes him. He conveniently doesnt underline the part that exposes and refutes his mutilated understanding.

Shaykh Al Islam said in the same scan this Madkhali Zindq provides:

So those people, if they know that it is not permissible for them to judge except by what Allah has revealed (here Ibn Taymiyyah establishes they dont do creedal Istihalal), yet they do not adhere to that (here Ibn Taymiyyah distinguishes between creedal belief, and not outwardly adhering to something); rather, they deem it halal (linguistically, i.e., Istihlal Amali) to judge contrary to what Allah has revealed  then they are disbelievers.

Istihlal Amali by consensus of Ahlul Sunnah isnt Kufr or Shirk, unless the action itself is inherently Kufr or Shirk. If this is merely about linguistic Istihlal to govern by man made laws, what do you think it is with legislating man made laws yourself? Did Ibn Taymiyyah make Tawbah from this Fatwa before he died? Keep in mind, Ibn Taymiyyah starts with There is no doubt, and this is a point of consensus amongst the classical Muslims. 

This moron Madkhali highlights the latter Istihlal, thinking its creedal, when the sentence before it explicitly negates that. This more or less, summaries how Madkhalis are with Daleel.

This is why, 99% of every Madkhali Shubuha can be refuted with simply possessing basic Arabic reading comprehension."

text:
"In defense of Shaykh Ibn Jibrn by the Grand Muft, Shaykh li al-Fawzn.

Q: May Allh bless you, this questioner says that some students of knowledge were happy at the death of Shaykh Ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him. Is this action correct?

Ans: This is not permissible. Rejoicing at the death of a Muslim in general is not permissible, because he is someone who has entered the protection of Islm, so how much more at the death of a scholar? How much more at the death of a scholar! It is not permissible for them to rejoice at the death of a scholar from the scholars of the Muslims. This only happens either out of ignorance, or because of hypocrisy, and we seek refuge with Allh. Yes.

Q: May Allh preserve you, O Shaykh, we see now on some social media platforms that some brothers, may Allh guide them, we see them speaking against major scholars such as Shaykh Abdullh ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, and Shaykh Abd al-Ramn al-Barrk, may Allh preserve him and prolong his life, attributing to them things Allh has sent down no authority for. They follow up their slips and say, These people said such-and-such, so their intention must be such-and-such. What is your guidance, may Allh preserve you, especially since they have many followers on these platforms?

Ans: This is not permissible at all. This is backbiting, from the forbidden backbiting, and backbiting the scholars is even more severe, and we seek refuge with Allh. It is not permissible to backbite the scholars or speak against them or follow up their mistakes. You must advise such people if you see them, or if any one of them is among you, then advise him regarding this. As the scholars have said: The flesh of the scholars is poisoned, their flesh is poisoned. So it is not permissible to backbite them or speak against them.

Q: May Allh be good to you, this question has been repeated often: there are those who attack some of the shaykhs, such as Shaykh Abdullh ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, and describe him with inappropriate descriptions. What is your direction for such people, and what is your advice for those who have made wounding the scholars and supplicators their occupation?

Ans: May Allh be good to you, these people harm no one but themselves; they do not harm the mashyakh. They harm none but themselves. Even if we suppose that some of the shaykhs have some error, if he is alive, he is advised; and if he is dead, he is asked forgiveness for, because he is your brother. You seek forgiveness for him and ask Allh to grant him forgiveness, reward, and recompense. Yes. May Allh be good to you.
 
Source:
 

We advise our brothers to fear Allh and to stop circulating claims that are baseless. They should also avoid exaggerating the status of certain individuals by giving them titles they neither merit nor have attained. This kind of ghuluw is harmful, and it only creates the false impression that the one making criticism is a scholar of tremendous authority, when that is simply not the case. Shaykh Muqbil, may Allh reward him for what he contributed, had his efforts in the field of adth and his role in reviving study among many students. However, he was not free of shortcomings, and it is well-known that his knowledge in usl al-fiqh, fiqh, and the broader Islamic sciences was limited compared to the major scholars of his time. Therefore, calling him Shaykh al-Islm is an exaggeration without evidence. In truth, Shaykh Ibn Jibrn, may Allh have mercy on him, was far ahead of him in jurisprudence, its principles, and in many other fields of knowledge. May Allh honor our scholars, reward them for their efforts, forgive their mistakes, and protect us from statements that cause division or unnecessary hostility within the Salaf community. mn."

Imam al-Safarrini al-Hanbali mentions:

text:
In a Al-Bukhr, from Ab Ubayd, the freed slave of Ibn Azhar, who said: I witnessed the two Eids with Uthmn ibn Affn on a Friday. He performed the prayer before delivering the sermon, then said: O people, today two Eids have coincided for you. Whoever among the residents of the outskirts (Al-awl) wishes to wait for the Jumu'ah, let him do so; whoever wishes to return home, I give him permission.

Based on these authentic prophetic traditions and reliable reports from the pious predecessors, the majority of scholars have concluded the following:

First: Whoever attends the Eid prayer is granted a concession from attending the Jumu'ah prayer. He may instead pray the Dhuhr prayer at its time. However, if he chooses to attend the Jumuah prayer with the congregation, that is more virtuous and better.

Second: Whoever did not attend the Eid prayer is not included in the concession. Therefore, the obligation of Jumuah remains upon him, and he must go to the masjid for the Jumuah prayer. If the minimum number required to establish Jumu'ah is not found, he performs Dhuhr instead.

Third: The Imm of the masjid is obliged to hold the Jumuah prayer that day, so that those who wish to attend it or those who did not attend the Eid prayer may do so. This is the explicit statement of the Prophet : Indeed, we are going to establish Jumuah prayer.

Fourth: Whoever attended the Eid prayer and avails himself of the concession not to attend Jumuah must pray Dhuhr. The Dhuhr prayer does not fall away under any circumstance, because what is lifted is the obligation of Jumuahnot of Dhuhr. Those upon whom Jumuah is not obligatorysuch as the sick, the traveller, women, and childrenare unanimously required by the scholars to perform Dhuhr.

Fifth: The opinion that whoever attends the Eid prayer is exempt from both the Jumuah and the Dhuhr prayers for that day is a weak opinion, though attributed to some Companions such as Ibn Az-Zubayr and Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them). This conclusion may be explained in several ways:

First, perhaps those who held this view were unaware of the adths that explicitly grant a concession from attending Jumuah, but affirm the obligation of Dhuhr for those who do not attend Jumuah.

Second, Ibn Az-Zubayr may have combined the Eid and Jumuah prayers at the time of the early Jumuah (before the sun reached its zenith). This is supported by a narration from Jbir ibn Abdullh (may Allh be pleased with him): We used to perform the prayer with the Messenger of Allh , then return and let our camels rest. This was reported by Muslim. Also, Sahl (may Allh be pleased with him) said: We would neither nap nor eat lunch until after Jumuah. This is the position of Imm Amad and Isq ibn Rhawayh (may Allh have mercy on them).

Additional evidence indicating that Ibn Az-Zubayr did this includes narrations such as: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the morning. We then went for Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we told him, he said: He followed the Sunnah. In another narration: A Friday and Eid coincided under Ibn Az-Zubayr, who said: Two Eids have come together in one day. So he combined them into one, prayed two rakahs early in the morning, and did not add anything until he prayed Ar.

This is a clear and explicit text that he included the Jumuah within the Eid prayer. And whoever performs the Jumuah does not then perform Dhuhr. Therefore, when this was reported to Ibn Abbs, he said: He acted upon the Sunnah.

This, dear brothers and sisters, is a concise yet beneficial explanation regarding the concurrence of Jumuah and Eid. I ask Allh, the Exalted, to have guided me to speak in accordance with the Sunnah and to have brought clarity on this issue to the general Muslim public...

text:
"The benefits of habitually listening to the Qurn when driving:

1. It will increase and strengthen your emn (Surat at-Tawbah: 124).
2. You will follow the Sunnah as the Messenger of Allah loved when the Quran was recited to him.
3. It will cause the Shaytan to flee from you.
4. It will take away the opportunity to commit sins (I.e. music, TikTok, Instagram, YouTube).
5. It will help you memorize and review what you have already memorized.
6. If you are studying Arabic language, it will aid you."

text:
Israeli Forces Tortured Child in Front of His Father in Gaza  

Israeli forces subjected a one-year-old child to abuse and torture to pressure his father into giving confessions in Al-Maghazi refugee camp in central Gaza, according to local and family sources.

Osama Abu Nassar, while taking his child to buy supplies a few days ago, he was caught in gunfire near his home and forced by Israeli soldiers to leave his 18-month-old son on the ground and approach the Israeli checkpoint, where he was stripped of his clothes.

According to witnesses, the army then took the child and interrogated the father at the checkpoint. The forces tortured the child in front of his father, including burning his leg with cigarettes, pricking, and inserting a metal nail into his leg, as confirmed by a medical report.

The child, Karim, was released after 10 hours and handed over to his family through the International Red Cross, while the father remains in detention.

 Osama Al-Kahlout

text:
"NEW: The confession of the perpetrator of the Tadamon Massacre, Amjad Yousesf:

 The individual appearing in the viral footage known as the Tadamon massacre is me, Amjad Youssef. These events date back to the early years of the Syrian revolution. 

 At the time, I was accompanied by Najib al-Halabi and a photographer whose name I cannot recall. We rounded up 40 individuals at random, under the pretext that they were 'terrorists'.

 We took them to a pre-dug pit, where I began executing them, as shown in the video. In some instances, I waited for the victim to fall into the pit before firing; in others, I shot them before they fell. 

 Intermittently, I would call on Najib al-Halabi to take his turn in the killing, until we had executed everyone present.

 We had previously placed car tires at the bottom of the pit. After the executions, we piled more tires over the bodies and set them on fire to prevent the scent of decomposition from spreading throughout the area. We then filled the pit. 

 I received no military orders to carry out this massacre, it was my own personal decision. 

 I cannot explain why there were exactly 40 victims, the selection was arbitrary, dictated solely by the circumstances at the time.

Source: Usman A Khan and translation by Syrian journalist Qusay Noor"

"We sends our condolences and prayers to the family of Ashfaq Ahmad. A truly one of a kind individual, who worked tirelessly for justice, particularly for his son, Babar.

text:
Ibn Rajab mentions in Ahwl al Qubr that some scholars mentioned that these 3 are singled out because:

The grave is the first stage of the Hereafter, offering a sample of the Day of Judgments consequences, not the full punishment/reward. 

Sins fall into two categories:
1.Rights of Allah (primary example: prayer)
2.Rights of servants (primary example: sanctity of life)

In the barzakh (the intermediate phase between death and Judgment), people are are not judged for the major sins themselves, but for their preludes and causes of lacking in them:

The prelude to prayer is to purify oneself of impurity and filth 

The prelude to violating the sanctity of life is slander and backbiting

So if a person failed to pray properly or violated the sanctity of a persons life, he will see the punishment for that on the Day of Judgment, but since these three (impurity, slander, backbiting) are lesser harms and foundational causes of these major sins, punishment in the grave begins with them; serving as an early consequence before the full reckoning on the Day of Judgment.

text:
""Tonight is the night most hoped to be Laylat al-Qadr.
Do not waste this night. Do not indulge in arm.
Stand before Allah in your alh and prolong your standing before Him. Increase in dhikr, alh, du, and the recitation of the Qurn.
Make du and pour your heart out to Allah. Do not hesitate for even a second.
May Allah grant us and you success."
"
--------------

Ramadhan 1447 (Day 27) 
The Night Most Hoped to Be Laylat Al-Qadr 

This is the view held by the majority of scholars, and it is considered the strongest and most well-known opinion. The evidence includes the following hadiths:

1 The Prophet  said:
((      ))
If someone is searching for it, let him look for it on the twenty-seventh night. [Sahih al-Jami, no. 2920] 

2 Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) reported that a man came to the Prophet  and said: O Messenger of Allah, I am an old and weak man, and standing in prayer at night is difficult for me. Tell me a night in which Allah may allow me to reach Laylat Al-Qadr. The Prophet  said:
(( ))
Focus on the seventh night (meaning the 27th). [Authenticated by Ibn Rajab in Lataif Al-Maarif and by Al-Arnaut in his checking of Musnad Ahmad] 

3 Muawiyah (may Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah  said:
((    ))
Laylat Al-Qadr is the twenty-seventh night. [Sahih Abi Dawud, no. 1386] 

4 Ubayy ibn Kab (may Allah be pleased with him) was certainhe even sworethat it was the 27th night. When he was asked how he knew this, he said: By the sign the Messenger of Allah  told us: the sun rises the next morning without strong rays. [Reported by Muslim, no. 762] 

5 The Prophet  led the companions in night prayer during some nights until one-third of the night had passed, and on some nights until half the night. But on the 27th night, he led them in prayer until morning. [Authenticated by al-Albani in As-Silsilah 7/101] 

6 On the 27th night, the Prophet  gathered his family and wives (unlike the 23rd and 25th nights) and prayed with them until they feared they might miss the pre-dawn meal (suhoor).
[Sahih Ibn Majah, no. 1100] 

 The 27th night begins at sunset of the 26th day of Ramadan.

We ask Allah to allow us and all Muslims to reach it and to be among those who stand in prayer during it with faith and sincere hope for reward.

Source:

text:
" Generosity in the Month of Raman

 Sh Abu Fuayl Abdur Raqb ( ).

 

 - Seeking nearness to Allh by way of giving in charity and being plentiful in generosity in this blessed month of Raman.
- Believe in Allh and His Messenger and spend out of that in which He has made you successors. For those who have believed among you and spent, there will be a great reward. [Srah al-Hadd 57:7]
- So fear Allh as much as you are able and listen and obey and spend [in the way of Allh]; it is better for your selves. And whoever is protected from the stinginess of his soul - it is those who will be the successful. [Srah at-Taghbun 64:16]
- Small deeds that will benefit us in the hereafter and protect us from the punishment of Allh.
- How Allh cultivates charity given from lawful earnings.
- So whoever does an atom's weight of good will see it. And whoever does an atom's weight of evil will see it. [Srah az-Zalzalah 99:7-8]
- The meaning of .
- A adth that highlights the outcome of a deed that emanates from fear of Allh, mercy, love or sincerity, even if it is small.
- Focusing on recitation and implementation of the Qurn in Raman.
- The reason for the Messengers  increased generosity in the month of Raman.
- Fruits of reciting and reflecting upon the Qurn.
- The Messengers  detachment from the duny.",

text:
""Iranians are exceptionally nationalistic about map boundaries compared to most populations, especially in the Middle East and other post-colonial regions.

We view our borders as ancient, not colonial, and take pride in the fact that they were not externally engineered in the twentieth century.

It is our civilizational inheritance.

It is not an overstatement to say that if Irans territorial integrity were ever seriously threatened, you would see monarchists, reformists, hardliners, etc., standing arm in arm to defend the country.

I have monarchist friends whom I know for a fact would get on a plane and risk imprisonment to offer themselves up to defend Iran.

For them, doing anything else would be unfathomable.

I feel the same way."
"
--------------

He speaks in a fictional we that exists only in Persian nationalist imagination, not in Irans lived reality. As a Sunni Iranian, this is immediately obvious, and it is obvious to anyone inside Iran who is not mainstream Persian. This includes even Shia groups who have largely lost a distinct identity and have been absorbed into Persian nationalism, such as many Azeris, Lors, and Caspian Iranians. 

This constant talk about Iran being uniquely special with natural borders is not some shared civilisational instinct. It is a standard modern Iranian nationalist myth, repeated so relentlessly that the average Iranian mistakes it for ancient truth, they actually believe they are special not realising they are not even the oldest civilisation in the Middle East. 

The irony is that both camps, i.e. Khomeinists and Shahists  that pretend to oppose each other fully embrace it Persian nationalist fairy tales. Yes, zealot hardcore  Khomeinists, the ideological heirs of the Safavids, and monarchists are united at the core. They quarrel over aesthetics, turbans versus crowns, but the substance is identical. Questioning the current borders is treated like apostasy, as if Iran itself were sacred. For many of them, it genuinely is, worshipped alongside God if not above Him (even in their everyday language one can observe that).

Everyone can destroy them with basic historical facts: the claim about ancient natural borders is laughable. It is pure myth making and cope on steroids. Geography has never magically produced legitimate states. Mountains and deserts were never sacred civilisational walls. They were zones of movement, trade, war, and overlap. The Zagros was a corridor for Arabs, Kurds, Lurs, Elamites (who were not Iranian in any ethnic sense), and Mesopotamian peoples for thousands of years. It was never a holy Iranian fence. Pretending otherwise is modern nationalist projection read backwards into history. But try to clarify these basics to them and they chimp out. 

Tey to explain to them that
Irans current borders did not descend from antiquity. They were violently hammered into shape under the  Safavids (Khomeini and Khamenei's salaf i.e. predecessors) and later reinforced by Qajar and Pahlavi centralisation through war, forced assimilation, and sectarian coercion as I already mentioned. Even when the rulers were Turco Persian, like the Safavids and Qajars, the project remained Persian nationalist and Shia supremacist, using sect and Persian identity to crush minorities, especially Sunnis. Borders shifted constantly and Sunnis and non Persian peoples were absorbed by force, not consent. For Sunnis, Kurds, Baloch, Arabs, and others, these borders were never inherited with pride. Never. They were imposed with blood, exclusion, and humiliation. This is precisely why both Khomeinists and Pahlavists are rightfully deeply resented among these communities.

What is telling is that this Iranian nationalist arrogance comes from insecurity. A multi ethnic state that does not match its ethnic or linguistic reality needs a myth of inevitability to survive. So conquest is rebranded as geography and coercion as civilisation. Older Middle Eastern peoples do not need this fantasy to justify themselves. Iranian nationalism does, because without it the entire story collapses.

The idea that monarchists, reformists, and hardliners would all unite to defend this map only proves the point. Different costumes, same ideology.

What makes this worse is not ignorant masses repeating these myths, but an Iranian elite that actively manufactures and teaches them to children as unquestionable fact. I heard these fairy tales constantly growing up, alongside open contempt for Arabs and Afghans and absurd fantasies about Iran belonging next to Austria and Switzerland. This is not pride. It is denial dressed up as history.,49.0

I have monarchist friends whom I know for a fact would get on a plane and risk imprisonment to offer themselves up to defend Iran. For them, doing anything else would be unfathomable. I feel the same way." " -------------- He speaks in a fictional we that exists only in Persian nationalist imagination, not in Irans lived reality. As a Sunni Iranian, this is immediately obvious, and it is obvious to anyone inside Iran who is not mainstream Persian. This includes even Shia groups who have largely lost a distinct identity and have been absorbed into Persian nationalism, such as many Azeris, Lors, and Caspian Iranians. This constant talk about Iran being uniquely special with natural borders is not some shared civilisational instinct. It is a standard modern Iranian nationalist myth, repeated so relentlessly that the average Iranian mistakes it for ancient truth, they actually believe they are special not realising they are not even the oldest civilisation in the Middle East. The irony is that both camps, i.e. Khomeinists and Shahists that pretend to oppose each other fully embrace it Persian nationalist fairy tales. Yes, zealot hardcore Khomeinists, the ideological heirs of the Safavids, and monarchists are united at the core. They quarrel over aesthetics, turbans versus crowns, but the substance is identical. Questioning the current borders is treated like apostasy, as if Iran itself were sacred. For many of them, it genuinely is, worshipped alongside God if not above Him (even in their everyday language one can observe that). Everyone can destroy them with basic historical facts: the claim about ancient natural borders is laughable. It is pure myth making and cope on steroids. Geography has never magically produced legitimate states. Mountains and deserts were never sacred civilisational walls. They were zones of movement, trade, war, and overlap. The Zagros was a corridor for Arabs, Kurds, Lurs, Elamites (who were not Iranian in any ethnic sense), and Mesopotamian peoples for thousands of years. It was never a holy Iranian fence. Pretending otherwise is modern nationalist projection read backwards into history. But try to clarify these basics to them and they chimp out. Tey to explain to them that Irans current borders did not descend from antiquity. They were violently hammered into shape under the Safavids (Khomeini and Khamenei's salaf i.e. predecessors) and later reinforced by Qajar and Pahlavi centralisation through war, forced assimilation, and sectarian coercion as I already mentioned. Even when the rulers were Turco Persian, like the Safavids and Qajars, the project remained Persian nationalist and Shia supremacist, using sect and Persian identity to crush minorities, especially Sunnis. Borders shifted constantly and Sunnis and non Persian peoples were absorbed by force, not consent. For Sunnis, Kurds, Baloch, Arabs, and others, these borders were never inherited with pride. Never. They were imposed with blood, exclusion, and humiliation. This is precisely why both Khomeinists and Pahlavists are rightfully deeply resented among these communities. What is telling is that this Iranian nationalist arrogance comes from insecurity. A multi ethnic state that does not match its ethnic or linguistic reality needs a myth of inevitability to survive. So conquest is rebranded as geography and coercion as civilisation. Older Middle Eastern peoples do not need this fantasy to justify themselves. Iranian nationalism does, because without it the entire story collapses. The idea that monarchists, reformists, and hardliners would all unite to defend this map only proves the point. Different costumes, same ideology. What makes this worse is not ignorant masses repeating these myths, but an Iranian elite that actively manufactures and teaches them to children as unquestionable fact. I heard these fairy tales constantly growing up, alongside open contempt for Arabs and Afghans and absurd fantasies about Iran belonging next to Austria and Switzerland. This is not pride. It is denial dressed up as history.,49.0

text:
"A Warning To Those That Defend Themselves But Are Silent When The Truth & It's People Are Violated 

Ibn Qayyim    said: 

"And what religion, and what good, is there in a person who sees that which Allah has prohibited being violated, His boundaries being ignored and transgressed upon, His religion being abandoned, and the Sunnah of His Messenger being rejected? Yet his heart is unmoved, and his tongue remains silent. Indeed, he is a mute devil, just like one who speaks with falsehood is an articulate devil. 

Has the religion (of Allah) ever been tested with anything other than the likes of these people? Those who, so long as their food and (positions of) leadership are secured and protected, have no concern for what happens to the religion of Allah. The best among them may feel slight sadness, but should he be challenged regarding something he holds dearwhether concerning his status or his wealthhe will strive and toil to the utmost of his ability, using the three levels of rejection and rebuke to defend himself, (i.e. they use their hands, their tongues, and their heart to; change, repel, reject, and rebuke that evil). 

These individuals, though they have fallen in the sight of Allah and are under His anger, have been tested in this worldly life with the greatest of trials, yet they do not feel it. Certainly, for sure, it is the death of the hearts. For indeed, the more alive the heart is, the stronger its anger is for the sake of Allah and His Messenger, and the more complete its defence of the religion shall be."

['Ilm al-Muwaqq'n 'an Rabb al-'lamn Ibn al-Qayyim 2/177] translated by Abu Ukkashah Abdulhakeem"

text:
"THIS IS NOT THE END

Shaykh Suliman al Alwaan (may Allah free him) said:

The fall of Dr al-Salm, Baghdad, and other Islamic lands is not the end of the world nor the end of Islam. Let the gloaters be disgraced. The future belongs to this deen, and soon the last part of this Ummah will be joined to its earliest part. So we must not weaken, nor should our efforts cease in supporting Muslims everywhere. Allah has praised those who face trials and hardship with steadfastness and patience. He said: And when the believers saw the confederates, they said: This is what Allah and His Messenger promised us, and Allah and His Messenger spoke the truth. And it increased them only in faith and submission.

These trials and hardships that befall the Muslims are a test, a means to distinguish the wicked from the good, the righteous from the corrupt, and the truthful from the liar. The believers increase in faith, while the hypocrites and those who worship Allah on the edge (I.e. in doubt) weaken, fall into cowardice, and show gloating. They say, If they had obeyed us, they would not have been killed. Allah the Exalted said: And among the people is he who worships Allah on an edge. If good befalls him, he is content with it; but if a trial touches him, he turns back on his face. He loses both this world and the Hereafter, that is the clear loss.

And He said: And among the people is he who says, We believe in Allah, but when he is harmed in Allahs cause, he considers the trial of the people as the punishment of Allah. But if victory comes from your Lord, they will surely say, Indeed, we were with you. Is not Allah most knowing of what is within the breasts of all beings?

And upon those whom Allah has entrusted with this great responsibility, such as the imams of mosques and others, is to urge the worshippers toward piety, repentance to Allah, giving them glad tidings of victory and empowerment, and making them aware of what the spiteful West intends, and what their brothers among the hypocrites, secularists, and the like are planning: a comprehensive assault on Islam and the Muslims, as they have openly declared a crusade.

They must work by every possible means to halt their advance and repel their aggression against the sanctities, wealth, and honor of the Muslims, and to strive against them with wealth, lives, and speech. For the Prophet  said: "Strive against the Mushrikeen with your wealth, your lives, and your tongues."
Reported by Imam Ahmad in his Musnad (12246), Ab Dwd (2504), and al-Nas (3098) through the narration of Hammad ibn Salamah, from Humayd, from Anas. Its chain is sound.

text:
(56)
Fond Memories 

In Dammaj, there were two main masjids: the central masjid, where all the students gathered for Sheikh Yahyas daily lessons, and another masjid located in the mazraah (farmlands). 

During Ramadhan, these two masjids would engage in a friendly competition to see who could complete the most khatmahs.

One night, during class, a note was presented to Sheikh Yahya informing him that the brothers in the mazraah had completed Surah Al-Baqarah, Aal Imran, and An-Nisaa all in a single night. After praising them and invoking blessings upon their efforts, the sheikh went on to clarify that the Prophet     recited all three of these Surahs in just one rakah. 

Source:

text:
"As Muslims, we must stand firm upon our faith and reject those who falsely claim to represent us. Iran, often portrayed as a defender of Muslim causes, cannot and must not be supported, because:

1 Their belief system has no connection to Islam. Their aqdah strays far from Tawd and slips into shirk, by elevating their Imams to near-divine status and seeking intercession through them in ways that clearly contradict the oneness of Allah. Their deep rooted hatred towards the Prophet Muammads (peace be upon him) noble companions and honorable wives, especially ishah (may Allah be Pleased with her), is a major red line that no sincere Muslim can ignore.

2 Iran has the blood of countless Muslims on its hands. In fact, its crimes in places like Syria and Yemen have caused more death and destruction than even Israel.

 Our stance is not to support either side. Rather, their internal conflict weakens their grip on the region while keeping them preoccupied with themselves. This may give our brothers and sisters in Gaza and elsewhere some breathing space and relief.

Some ask: Why not support Iran, when the so-called Sunni Arab countries are doing nothing for Gaza?
The truth is, even these regimes have nothing to do with Islam. They are secular puppets of America and Israel. The ordinary people (awm) in these nations are either powerless, or live in fear, trapped behind blocked nationalistic borders. Their rulers serve foreign interests. Not the Ummah.

Had these countries been ruled by sincere Muslims, united in Tawd and loyalty to Allah, the Zionist entity Israel would have collapsed long ago. Instead, they remain idle while Gaza bleeds proving their betrayal beyond any doubt.

As Muslims, we must reject both Irans deviant sectarian agenda and the treachery of pro-Western regimes.",

Marriage, wealth, health, Hajj, death, food, clothing - everything.

On Laylat al-Qadr, the decrees for the coming year are transferred from al-Lawh al-Mahfooz.

 "Is Laylat al-Qadr in every Ramadan?"

text:
Ibn Qudmah   said : 

Some of the righteous predecessors would dig a grave for themselves. Whenever they became fatigued from performing righteous actions, they would lower themselves into the grave, stretch out, and say to themselves:

'O my soul, imagine that you are dead and now in the grave. What would you wish for?'

The soul would respond, 'I would wish to return to the duny (worldly life) so that I may perform righteous actions.'

The person would reply, 'You have been granted what you wished for. So get up and do righteous actions (before it is too late).

[Wasiyah 11]

text:
"Jam'at Ahl as-Sunnah lid-Da'wah wa'l-Jihd (JASDJ), mostly known as Boko Haram, recently released a video threatening ISWAP. The group in the video appears to be based in the Lake Chad area under the authority of the JAS overall leader Bakura Doro, aka Abu Umaymah.    

The speaker begins by calling ISWAP apostates and deviants, and tells them not to rejoice over the death of Abu Bakr al-Shekawi (Shekau). As a reminder, Shekau blew himself up in 2021 when he was cornered by ISWAP in the Sambisa Forest. ISWAP had called upon him with loudspeakers to surrender, but he refused.    

He adds that after Shekau there are many other Shekaus who dont die and dont change, and who follow his ways and teachings, and will remain until the Day of Judgement.    

He calls ISWAP deviants for not considering regular Muslim civilians apostates for living in territories controlled by the Nigerian government, which JAS describes as the general of the polytheists. This is a major difference between JAS (Boko Haram) and ISWAP, as the latter only sees the military, their auxiliaries, and Christians who dont pay the jizyah as legitimate targets. By contrast, JAS views everyone living under the apostate governments as legitimate targets, which is the reason behind the recent bombings in Maiduguri by JAS.    

The speaker then addresses ISWAP again, urging them to repent before JAS destroys them, and says that if they do, they will be safe from them.  

He then greets JAS fighters in all their provinces, the Mandara Mountains, and Sadikus mobile faction in the northwest and north central, as they renew their pledge of allegiance to Abu Umaymah. Children are visible among the group."
