text:
" 

Asalamualaikum wa rahmatullahi wa barakaatuhu 

We hope and pray you are in the best of eemaan. The masjid has some great news to share, and we pray this assists in us pushing on and spreading the dawa more throughout Manchester. 

We are in the process of finalising a lease on a brand-new bookshop and dawa centre. Its not been easy to find something but we have tried to find the best place possible to accommodate both enough books, free literature as well as allowing small sittings and potentially lessons in the future by taken adjacent buildings.

We will announce the location in due course due to the sensitive nature of negotiations. However as we are at the stage of agreeing terms we thought to bring this to your attention so that you may contribute to such a blessed dawa centric activity. 

Sahl bin Sa'd (May Allah be pleased with him) reported: The Prophet () said to 'Ali (May Allah be pleased with him), "By Allah, if a single person is guided by Allah through you, it will be better for you than a whole lot of red camels." Recorded in Al-Bukhari and Muslim (Riyad as-Salihin 1379).

In order to set up the bookshop we estimate that it will cost us approximately 42,000. This includes the legal fees, deposit on the building, the full refurbishment costs (electrical, plumbing, lighting, flooring, ceiling) furniture fixings, signage (external and internal) and then most important of all the books to stock and dawa material; as well as further stock to sell in order to raise funds to sustain the centre.

Please note our goal is only to make enough money so that it can carry out its main purpose, which is to call to Islam. Alongside the weekly city centre dawa stall we have, this will hopefully form a good central hub for people who are maybe new to Islam or want more information.
 
So we ask that you donate generously if you are able, to this noble cause of spreading Islam and teaching it to people - and rectifying the basics of the religion and assisting Muslims all over Manchester to return to the sunnah.

 :
MANCHESTER BOOKSTORE LTD
Account number: 21981312
Sort code: 04-06-05

 :


:
Manchesterbookstore@outlook.com

  -

""Islam Makhachev met with Serbian President Aleksandar Vui and gifted him the gloves from the fight that made him a two-time champion. 

President Vui said:
"It was an exceptional pleasure and privilege to host @MAKHACHEVMMA , an outstanding athlete, a world champion, and by far the most popular and strongest man I have ever received.
The gloves he gave me are from his last fight, when he became a two-time champion and the greatest of all time.

We talked about the importance of sport as the foundation of a healthy society and about the role that top athletes play in motivating young people to develop discipline, responsibility, and perseverance. Serbia continues to support sport and young talents, because sport promotes true values, mutual respect, and connects people all over the world.

I wished Islam to continue winning and to achieve his biggest dreams, both in the ring and in life.""
"

text:
Either Yahya Silmi received this statement from idle, malicious opponents of the call of Imam Muqbil ibn Hadi Al-Wadii, which has filled the plains and mountains, or he heard something similar to what Abdullah Al-Bukhari statedthat Sheikh Muqbil repented from the methodology of khuruj in the sense of his words two months before his deathand due to his poor command of Arabic and ignorance, he assumed they were accusing Sheikh Muqbil of saying that Saudi Arabia is a kafir country. It is sufficient to show a persons misguidance that he be a liar in what he transmits or inarticulate in his speech, for the inarticulate cannot express what is in his heart nor clarify what he intends, thus falling into something worse than what he wishes to deny. His situation is like what some of the Salaf said about Umar ibn Ubayd: Your shortcoming came from your poor Arabic, when he said, It is incumbent upon Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, to fulfill the threat. So I advise Yahya Silmi to fear Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, and I advise him to be humble and to seek knowledge.

The second matter: His statement, He sat with its scholars and they convinced himwhat did they convince him of? The fact is that it was conveyed to him, may Allah have mercy on him, that there was some injustice in Saudi Arabia and such things, so he would condemn that specific injustice that was reported to him; otherwise, he remained upon the Salafi creed. Evidence for this is that Sheikh Ibn Baz, Sheikh Ibn Uthaymin, Sheikh Rabee, Sheikh An-Najmi, and Sheikh Al-Albaninone of them, not in a single letter, accused Sheikh Muqbil of holding the opinion of the Khawarij or the takfiris, despite their knowledge of what he said or uttered, whether in what he wrote in Gharat Al-Ashirat or what he wrote in Al-Makhraj min Al-Fitnah or what he stated in other books. Then he retracted, may Allah have mercy on him, before his death; he retracted from all statements he had made about Saudi Arabia in that tape entitled My Observations in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia when he saw and heard the good in the country, as he explained the reason for his retraction.

The point of this introduction is: I declare this transmission and this statement from Yahya Silmi to be false. Sheikh Muqbil did not declare the Saudi government to be disbelievers nor did he say it is a kafir country. Scholars condemned Sayyid Qutb when he called it the Jahiliyyah of the twentieth century, and then Yahya Silmi comes and attributes such a statement to an imam of the Salafi call and a reviver of the Prophetic Sunnah, claiming that he used to say Saudi Arabia is a disbelieving country. So check your sources, O you who fabricates! 
{ } 
Cursed be the liars! 
Review the Arabic language and master it. If something is unclear to you, be humble and ask until you understand. But to become a fabricator who falsely attributes statementsand to whom?! To an imam known for the soundness of his creed and methodology! We did not accept from Abdullah Al-Bukhari nor from anyone else who claims that Sheikh Muqbil repented from khuruj. We say that Sheikh Muqbil never held the view of khuruj, nor do we accept that, neither at the beginning of his pursuit of knowledge nor at the end. Sheikh Muqbil was a Sunni Salafi from the time Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, enlightened him with the Book and the Sunnah. He denied this about himself, truthfully in what he said and conveyed, that he was never with Juhayman at any time, nor was he a supporter of him; rather, he condemned him, and so on. I refer to what I wrote in my book Awn Al-Bari fi Bayan Hizbiyyati Ibnay Mari wa Man Jara Majrahum wa Radd Takharusat Abdullah Al-Bukhari, and added to it is the refutation of the fabrications of Yahya Silmi Al-Saylani.

text:
"The Islamic Emirate of Afghanistan has taken a decisive step, dismantling the interest-based banking system and replacing it entirely with an Islamic financial framework.

Officials from Da Afghanistan Bank confirmed that the nations financial institutions are being cleansed of usury, realigning the economy with the Islamic Shariah.

text:
"Yine bir kemalist zihniyetin zulm!

7 yanda bir kz renci, 22 Nisanda okulun son saatinde tren iin dar arlyor. renci trene katlmak istemediini ve insan suretinin olmas nedeniyle orada bulunmak istemediini sylemitir. Devamnda bayraa ve stiklal Marna saygl olduunu ifade etmitir. Dini inanc gerei insan suretinin nnde durmamas gerektiini ve ailesinin de bu tr eylerden holanmadn sylemitir.

Bunun zerine nbeti retmen durumu erkek mdr yardmcsna iletmi ve mdr yardmcs kzn kolundan tutup zorla tren alanna karmtr. Burada mdr yardmcs birok su ilemitir:

* ocuun beden dokunulmazln ihlal etmitir
* Cebir ve zor kullanmtr
* Kii zgrln kstlamtr
* Grevini ktye kullanmtr
* ocua psikolojik bask uygulamtr

Anayasamz kendi iinde elimektedir. Din ve inan hrriyeti maddesi vardr. Bu maddenin kapsam, bir kiinin Kurana ve hadislere iman etmesi ve emir ile yasaklarna uymasdr. rnek olarak bir renci teneffste namaz klmak isterse ve retmen buna izin vermezse bu su oluturur. Ayn ekilde insan suretinin nnde sayg duruunda bulunmak inancna gre yasak ise kimse bu yasa inemesi iin zorlayamaz.

Bu sadece Mustafa Kemal iin deil, kim olursa olsun ayndr. Gerekse Fatih Sultan Mehmet olsun, yine de fikri deimeyecektir. nk bu durum siyasi deil, dini bir inancn gerekliliidir.

Suret hakknda sahih hadisler ve ayetler vardr:

* Melekler, iinde kpek ve suret bulunan eve girmez. (Buhr, Mslim)
* Kyamet gn en iddetli azaba urayacak olanlar, suret yapanlardr. (Buhr, Mslim)
* Bu suretleri yapanlara kyamet gn azap edilecek ve Yarattklarnza can verin denilecektir. (Buhr, Mslim)

Objektif olmak gerekirse, kimisine gre lkeyi o kurtard, cumhuriyeti o kurdu; kimisine gre sarayn emriyle Samsuna kt, cumhuriyeti kurarken meclisi aniden toplayp dier vekillerin meclise gelmesini engelledi vs. Bu tartmalar bir kenara brakarak unu sylemek istiyorum:

Daha birka gn nce kck rencilerin arafn kt olduu ve mini etein uygar olduu el kadar ocuklara retilmeye alldn grdk. Kadnlara zgrlk bizim zamanmzda verildi diyerek rtl kadnlarn retmen olma zgrln acmaszca alabildiniz. Kurtulu savanda Mustafa Kemalin arkasnda Arap askerler vard grmediniz. Kurtulu savanda Krt kardelerimiz vard, sava sonrasnda bombalar Krtlere attnz. Kurtulu savanda cepheye yardm gtren arafl annelerimiz vard, imdi ayplyorsunuz.

Alkol alp, LGBTyi savunup, mini etek giymeyi uygarlk sanyorsunuz ok yazk. 

Yeniden dirili balad  !"

To add one more thing: Iranian nationalists always ignore is a map they can never answer. The average Iranian is not even aware of this basic fact, and once you see it, the fairy tale of a uniquely ordained Iran with natural borders uniting Aryans since antiquity collapses. But as a starter, Iranian speaking peoples did not even originate on the Iranian plateau at all. They migrated into it. Before them, the Elamites were already there, non Iranian in language and identity, with a civilisation LONG predating the arrival of Iranians. The same applies to Kurds, who long predate the modern Iranian state and were later absorbed into a Persian nationalist project they never consented to.
The geology alone destroys the myth. The Arabian plate penetrates deep into what is now called Iran. Ahwaz, historically known as Arabistan even under the Safavids, sits on the Arabian plate, not the Iranian microcontinent. That is why its climate, landscape, and historical orientation align with Mesopotamia and Arabia, not the Persian heartlands. This is not ideology. It is geology. These are factsz simple as.
The same fantasy is imposed on Kurdish lands. The Zagros was never a sacred Persian boundary. It was a zone of movement and autonomy for Kurds and other highland peoples, later subdued through conquest and centralisation.

So when Iranian nationalists talk about natural borders, what they really mean is simple. These are territories they conquered early enough that they now pretend geography itself demanded it. This is not destiny. It is conquest rewritten as nature.,50.0

text:
"A Reminder of Fairness and Justice in Defending the People of Knowledge
 
Among the first to be targeted with attacks and assaults on his status by Amjad Rafq was the noble Shaykh, the Faqh Araft al-Muammad (may Allh preserve him).
 
By the praise of Allh, his advice and refutations bore fruit, and today you can see the reality of what he had warned against, in what Amjad Rafq is now displaying of transgressions towards the status of knowledge and its people.
 
I wish to draw attention to a matter that may go unnoticed in the writings of some of the brothers and callers in the West:
 
Despite what we see, and it is commendable, of their responses to Amjad Rafqs attacks on Shaykh al-Allmah Abdullh al-Bukhr (may Allh preserve him), we hardly see a similar defence of their brother, Shaykh Araft al-Muammad (may Allh preserve him).
 
Even though Amjad Rafq had previously attacked him, accused him of lying, attributed to him weakness in knowledge, and described him with offensive terms, many of them did not move to refute this falsehood or stop this transgression.
 
It would have been more fitting, in upholding justice and fairness, that they also defend the honour of Shaykh Araft (may Allh preserve him), as he was among the first to confront the errors of Amjad Rafq and warn against his methodology.

Written by: Jafar al-Hshim al-Awz
@Arafatbinhassan @ZubayrAbbasi @yasin_ibn_jamal @AbooSuhailah"

text:
"Who was ash-Shaykh, al-Allmah, Ubayd al-Jbir?

He is the Shaykh and Allmah, Ubayd bin Abd Allh bin Sulaymn al-Jbir al-Hamadn, and Ban Jbir is among the tribes of arb of the ijz.
He was born in the town of al-Faqr in al-Far valley in the region of Madnah in 1357 AH (1938). At the age of eight, he moved with his father to Mahd al-Dhahab, where he acquired the first stages of his education. His father moved there in order to work. He remained in education at this location for eight years. After that, he returned to al-Far valley. Then in 1376 AH (1956) he settled in Madnah and, due to family circumstances, was unable to study for a period of time.

Then in the year 1381 AH (1961), he resumed his studies, starting with Dr al-adth al-Madaniyyah. Then he joined al-Mahad al-Ilm and stayed there for five years. He got married before joining the Faculty of Sharah in the Islamic University of Madnah in 1388 AH (1968). He graduated four years later in 1392 AH (1972), excelling in his grade.
From his teachers through the course of his studies include: Shaykh Sayf al-Ramn bin Amad, Shaykh Ammr bin Abd Allh, Shaykh Abd al-Ramn bin Abd Allh al-Ajln, Shaykh umayd al-zim, Shaykh ammd al-Anr and Shaykh Abd al-Musin al-Abbad.

After that, he was an imm in Masjid al-Sabt in Madnah between 1387-1392 AH. Then he was a teacher at Umar bin Abd al-Aziz Intermediate School in Jeddah from 1392 AH to 1396 AH. After that, the Shaykh worked as a caller for Markaz al-Dawah wal-Irshd, assisting the director of the centre, Shaykh Abd al-Azz bin Bz, in his presence and acting on his behalf in his absence from the end of 1396 AH to 1404 AH (1976-1983).
Then he was a teacher at the Islmic University between the end of 1407 AH to 1414 AH. During his presence there, he also attained a Masters in the subject of tafsr. After a few years serving at the Community Service Centre affiliated with the Islmic University, the Shaykh then retired in 1417 AH (1996).

After this the Shaykh continued his lectures and lessons, with students from the Islmic University frequenting his home and benefiting from his knowledge, advice and wisdom. During this period, the Shaykh also aided the Salafi dawah tremendously, particularly in the West by way of fatherly advice, clarifications, admonitions and refutations of the people of falsehood and their false innovated principles. The Shaykh had a very strong relationship with the students of knowledge from Maktabah Salafiyyah, Birmingham. The Shaykh has many beneficial works in the field of aqdah, manhaj, and fiqh. Shaykh Rab bin Hd (may Allh have mercy on him) said of the Shaykh: Shaykh Ubayd is from the esteemed scholars of the Salafs, and he is known for piety, abstinence and speaking with truth.

Shaykh Ubayd was also praised and commended by other Major Scholars such as Shaykh Amad al-Najm and Shaykh Zayd al-Madkhal (may Allh have mercy on them).
The Shaykh (may Allh have mercy on him) passed away at the age of 84 on 28 Rab al-Thn 1444 AH, corresponding to 22 November 2022. May Allh shower him with mercy and reward him greatly for his service to Islm, the Muslims and the Salaf dawah.

text:
Doubt: Shaykh Muqbil ibn Haadi, was a takfiri or made Takfir on Saudi

Responded by his student, Shaykh AbdulHameed az-Zukuri  :

Yahya Silmi As-Saylani (the Sri Lankan) says:
Sheikh Muqbils initial opinion, which caused a great uproar, was: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is a kafir country. He was firmly set on this statement, announcing it in Friday sermons. Then, when he went to Saudi Arabia before his death, he sat with its scholars, and they convinced him that it is a Salafi country, so he retracted and acknowledged that it is a pure Muslim country. [From the tape: Explanation of My Advice to Ahlus-Sunnah].

Sheikh Abdulhamid Az-Zukari responds:
So on Friday, the twenty-fifth of Rajab, in the year 1433 AH, we respond to this false statement, falsely and aggressively attributed to our sheikh, our father, and our teacher, Imam Muqbil ibn Hadi Al-Wadii, may Allah Almighty have mercy on himthe one whom Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, blessed and favoured by making him a reviver of this religion alongside other revivers, such as Sheikh Al-Albani, may Allah have mercy on him, Sheikh Ibn Baz, may Allah have mercy on him, and Sheikh Al-Uthaymin, may Allah have mercy on him. We have spoken about his way, his methodology, his biography, and some of what he adhered to in our book Al-Bayan Al-Hasan fi Tarjamat Al-Imam Al-Wadii wa Ma Ahyahu min As-Sunan. And we have responded to those who claim that Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, was a takfiri at any time or period in the book Awn Al-Bari li Bayan Hizbiyyati Ibnay Mari wa Man Jara Majrahum wa Radd Takharusat Abdullah Al-Bukhari. In that book, we explained the gist: that Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, was never a Haruri or a Khariji; rather, he was a Sunni Salafi ever since Allah, may He be glorified and exalted, blessed him with the knowledge of the creed of the Salaf, Ahlus-Sunnah wal-Jamaah. Thus, he became a caller to it and a reviver of it. 

Among the evidence that shows the falsehood of Yahya Silmis statementthat Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, said about the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia that it is a kafir countryare the following matters:

First: Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah Almighty have mercy on him, refuted Osama ibn Laden, refuted Abu Muhammad Al-Maqdisi, refuted Al-Masari, and spoke against Salman, Safar, and those similar to them who hold takfiri statements and deviant methodologies.

Second: We are certain and convinced that our sheikh, may Allah have mercy on him, never said this statement at any time because it contradicts reality, and Sheikh Muqbil was among the people most aware of the condition of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabiamay Allah grant it success and protect it with Tawhid.

Third: Sheikh Muqbil, may Allah have mercy on him, did not apply this description to countries that are less upright and less devoted to spreading good, let alone countries containing graves and shrines worshipped besides Allah, despite his belief that those who engage in grave-worship, those who call upon another god alongside Allah, those who slaughter for other than Allah, and those who make vows for other than Allah are disbelievers.

Fourth: Such an absolute statement is questionable. Even if we were to suppose that in that country there are acts of disbelief, or in Yemen there are acts of disbelief, or in Sudan there are acts of disbelief, or in Iraq there are acts of disbelief, this kind of absolute statementcalling any of the Muslim countries a kafir country outrightis questionable. This claim is contradicted by Sheikh Muqbils tapes, contradicted by the actual stance of Sheikh Muqbil, and contradicted by the students of Sheikh Muqbil.

Fifth: If Sheikh Muqbil had ever uttered this statement or something similar, the scholars would have condemned him for it.

text:
"How the Najdi scholars refuted the extremists

In the name of Allah, the Most Merciful, the Bestower of Mercy.

From: Abd al-Laf ibn Abd al-Ramn ibn asan
To: Abd al-Azz al-Khab

Peace be upon those who follow guidance, and upon the righteous servants of Allah.

To proceed:

I have read your letter and understood its contents, and what you intended by way of apology. However, you erred in your statement that what our shaykh, the father, denounced, namely your declaring the people of truth to be disbelievers, and your belief that you are correct, when you denied it.

You mentioned that your brothers from al-Naq are disputing with you and are arguing with you concerning us, and that they say that we have been silent regarding certain matters. And you know that they often mention this to attack our creed and to criticize our methodology. Even if they do not explicitly make Takfeer, they have approached its borders. So we seek refuge in Allah from misguidance after being upon guidance, from deviating away from the path of rectitude, and from blindness.

I witnessed, in the year 64, two men resembling you who were among the renegades in al-As. They abandoned the Friday prayer and the congregational prayer, and declared the Muslims of that land to be disbelievers. Their argument was similar to yours. They said: The people of al-As sit with Ibn Fayrz and mix with him and others like him, those who did not reject the ght by disbelieving in it, nor explicitly declare his grandfather to be a disbeliever who rejected the call of Shaykh Muammad, and did not accept it, and instead opposed it.

They both said: Whoever does not explicitly declare him to be a disbeliever is himself a disbeliever in Allah and has not rejected the ght by disbelieving in it. And whoever sits with him is like him. And upon these two false and misguided premises, they built rulings implying clear apostasy, such that they even abandoned returning greetings of Salam.

Their matter was raised to me, so I summoned them, threatened them, and spoke harshly to them. At first, they claimed that they were upon the creed of Shaykh Muammad ibn Abd al-Wahhb, and that his letters were with them. So I clarified their errors and refuted their misconceptions with what I had in that gathering.

I informed them that Shaykh Muammad is free from this belief and this path, and that he does not declare anyone to be a Kaafir except for those matters that the Muslims have unanimously agreed upon that makes one a Kaafir, such as major shirk, or rejecting the verses of Allah and His messengers, or any of that, after the proof has been shown to him and is properly conveyed. Such as making Takfeer on the one who worships the righteous, prays to them alongside Allah, and for making them His rivals in what He alone deserves in worship and authority over the creation.

This is agreed upon by the people of knowledge and faith. Every group among the followers of the various upheld Mazhabs has devoted a major chapter to this issue, mentioning its rulings, and that which causes apostasy and its implications, and explicitly mentioning Shirk. Ibn ajar even singled out this issue in a book he titled: al-Ilm bi-Qawi al-Islm (Informing about the Nullifiers of Islam).

These two Persians later showed repentance and remorse and claimed that the truth had become clear to them. Then they went to the coast and returned to that same doctrine. It reached us that they declared the leaders of the Muslims to be Kaafirs for communicating with the Egyptian rulers. Rather, they even declared as disbelievers those among the Muslim scholars who associated with those who communicated with them. We seek refuge in Allah from misguidance after being upon guidance and from relapse after rectitude.

We have also been informed about similar matters regarding you. And that you ....

Continued  "

text:
Shaykh ammd Al-Anr (may Allh have mercy on him) said:

When I arrived at a place near Makkah, I met some Bedouins and asked their leader: What are the pillars of Islam?

He replied, Ash-Shfi, Ab anfah, Mlik  and I forgot the fourth one!

So I said to those around him, Remind him  mention the fourth.

They said, We have forgotten.

I then said to them, Amad (ibn anbal).

They all exclaimed together, Yes, thats the one!

He added:

This happened when we went out for dawah in the year 1375 AH.

[Collected in his biography]

text:
"The West must dismantle the Muslim Brotherhood in full, not just monitor it, not just debate it. Dissolve it. Shut down its networks, charities, and political fronts.

Countries like Ireland, the UK, the US, France, and Germany must follow the example set by the UAE and Jordan. These arent just foreign policy moves, theyre acts of national defense.

Every day the Muslim Brotherhood is allowed to operate is a day it recruits, infiltrates, and rewrites the rules from within. This is not an organization with a place in civil society. It is a threat engineered to exploit it.

The UAE labeled the Muslim Brotherhood a terrorist organization and removed it from every institution, educational, religious, financial, and civic. Jordan went even further: the Brotherhood formally dissolved itself after losing all political and legal ground. These were not gestures. They were deliberate state decisions to eliminate the roots of radicalism before they could bloom into violence.

The same clarity is needed across the West, before the same patterns take hold in new soil",

text:
"Al-Fudayl ibn Iyad, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
If you see oppressors taking revenge on other oppressors, stand and observe in astonishment.

When you see the greatest enemies of Islam in this era, those with the most blood of Muslims on their hands, turning against one another and weakening themselves through their conflicts, let your heart be soothed and turn to Allah in gratitude.

Sometimes, Allah soothes hearts by letting oppressors and tyrants orchestrate their own downfall through their conflicts and wars.

We make the wrongdoers destructive to one another because of their misdeeds. (6:129)

The parties involved in this war, the US along with their awliy, the leaders of the Gulf, as well as the Rafidah and the Yahud, are enemies, equally guilty and steeped in crimes against Islam and Muslims. Their hands are stained with the blood of countless innocents, and to true believers who grasp the depth of their kufr and the enormity of their crimes, each is as condemned as the next, none surpassing the other in guilt. They vied ruthlessly in their oppression and in betraying the Muslims.

Malik ibn Dinar, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
"I read in the revealed Books of Allah that Allah, the Exalted, said: I destroy My enemies through My enemies, and then I destroy them through My awliy."

May Allah prolong their strife so that they are preoccupied with one another and weakened, and may He raise to leadership on this earth the Muslims they betrayed, oppressed, and harmed, so that Tawheed may be established across the earth!

May Allah safeguard every innocent Muslim wherever this conflict occurs!

Ahmad Musa Jibril
2 Shawwal 1447 AH"

text:
""Shaykh Rab al-Madhkal:

Shaykh al-Fawzn has already answered this question for you may Allah reward him with good. 

A student in the beginning of his path (of seeking knowledge) starts out as a Muqallid, if he does not perform taqld of Ahmad or al-Shfi, then makes taqld of his Ustdh.

Does the student not begin by making taqld of his teacher? He comes across a text and does not understand it so his teacher explains it to him. Has he not then done taqld of him?

So a person begins as a Muqallid, either of Abu Hanfah, or al-Shfii or Ahmad, or the teacher of his subject,  Then when he becomes knowledgeable and is able to research the evidence, he takes the soundest view (according to his own efforts) 

[Kitb Majm kutub wa-rasil wa fatwa]"
"
--------------

Shh Walullh ad-Dihlaw   
Know that before the fourth century, people were not unanimous in their adherence to a single, specific Madhab.
[   26p V1]

"Imam al-Dhahabi: May Allah Curse Those Who Spread Slander and Namimah

Certain individuals conveyed false reports to Imam Ibn Khuzaymah concerning some of his companions and students, leading him to declare them liars and to warn against them on the basis of what had reached him.

After mentioning this story and what Ibn Khuzaymah said about them, Imam al-Dhahabi commented: 

They are not liars; rather, they are reliable Imams. The Shaykh only spoke according to what was conveyed to him about them. So may Allah curse those who spread slander, and those who go about with namimah (tale-carrying).

(Siyar Alam al-Nubala 14/380)"

text:
"The Difference Between Dr al-Islm and Dr al-Kufr:

Ab Ysuf, the companion of Ab anfah, said:

A land is considered Dr al-Islm when the rulings of Islam are manifest in it, even if most of its inhabitants are disbelievers. And a land is considered Dr al-Kufr when the rulings of disbelief are manifest in it, even if most of its inhabitants are Muslims.
[al-Mabs by al-Sarakhs 10/144]

Ibn Mufli al-anbal said:

Every land in which the rulings of the Muslims prevail is Dr al-Islm, and if the rulings of disbelief prevail in it, then it is Dr al-Kufr. There is no third category beyond these two.
[al-db al-Shariyyah 1/213]

Ibn al-Qayyim al-Jawziyyah said:

The majority said: Dr al-Islm is that land in which the Muslims reside and upon which the rulings of Islam are implemented. Whatever the rulings of Islam are not implemented upon is not Dr al-Islm, even if it is adjacent to it.
[Akm Ahl al-Dhimmah 1/266]

Al-Q Ab Yal al-Farr said:

Every land in which the dominance is for the rulings of disbelief over the rulings of Islam is Dr al-Kufr.
[al-Mutamad f Ul al-Dn, p. 276]

Al-Ksn said:

When the rulings of disbelief become manifest in a land, it becomes Dr al-Kufr; a land becomes Dr al-Kufr by the manifestation of the rulings of disbelief.
[Badi al-ani 9/4375]

Muammad ibn al-asan al-Shaybn said:

Every land is attributed either to Islam or to disbelief. A land is attributed to Islam when its rulings are implemented in it, and it is attributed to disbelief when its rulings are implemented in it. Just as one says Paradise is the abode of peace and Hell is the abode of ruin, due to the presence of peace in Paradise and ruin in Hell. Likewise, the manifestation of Islam or disbelief is by the manifestation of their rulings.
[Badi al-ani 9/4375]

Manr al-Buht said:

Hijrah is obligatory upon one who is unable to manifest his religion in Dr al-arb, which is the land where the rulings of disbelief prevail.
[Kashshf al-Qin 3/43]

Al-Allmah amad ibn Atq said:

Whoever has familiarity with what the verifying scholars have established knows that when shirk becomes manifest in a land, prohibited matters are openly practiced, and the symbols of the religion are abolished, then it becomes a land of disbelief, its peoples wealth may be taken and their blood made lawful. The people of this land have gone further by openly insulting Allah and His religion, and they have established laws which they enforce upon the people, in opposition to the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of His Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam). And you know that this alone is sufficient to expel the one who does it from Islam.
[al-Durar al-Saniyyah 9/257]

The Mufti Muammad ibn Ibrhm l al-Shaykh said:

A land in which judgment is made by man-made law is not a land of Islam, and migration from it is obligatory. Likewise, if idoltary becomes manifest without being condemned or changed, then migration is obligatory. Disbelief, by its widespread prevalence and open manifestation, renders it a land of disbelief.
[Fatw Muammad ibn Ibrhm 6/188]"

Likewise, the manifestation of Islam or disbelief is by the manifestation of their rulings. [Badi al-ani 9/4375] Manr al-Buht said: Hijrah is obligatory upon one who is unable to manifest his religion in Dr al-arb, which is the land where the rulings of disbelief prevail. [Kashshf al-Qin 3/43] Al-Allmah amad ibn Atq said: Whoever has familiarity with what the verifying scholars have established knows that when shirk becomes manifest in a land, prohibited matters are openly practiced, and the symbols of the religion are abolished, then it becomes a land of disbelief, its peoples wealth may be taken and their blood made lawful. The people of this land have gone further by openly insulting Allah and His religion, and they have established laws which they enforce upon the people, in opposition to the Book of Allah and the Sunnah of His Prophet (salallahu alayhi wa salam). And you know that this alone is sufficient to expel the one who does it from Islam. [al-Durar al-Saniyyah 9/257] The Mufti Muammad ibn Ibrhm l al-Shaykh said: A land in which judgment is made by man-made law is not a land of Islam, and migration from it is obligatory. Likewise, if idoltary becomes manifest without being condemned or changed, then migration is obligatory. Disbelief, by its widespread prevalence and open manifestation, renders it a land of disbelief. [Fatw Muammad ibn Ibrhm 6/188]"

text:
"Ben Mermarli, lawyer of Marwan Barghouti, writes:

On April 12, 2026, I visited Marwan Barghouti in my capacity as his lawyer.

What I confirmed during this visit is deeply alarming. In recent weeks, Marwan Barghouti has been subjected to three violent assaults. On April 8, in Ganot Prison, he was severely beaten and left bleeding for more than two hours. He requested medical care, but his request was denied. On March 25, he was assaulted while being transferred from Megiddo to Ganot. And on March 24, in Megiddo Prison, guards entered his cell accompanied by a dog, forced him to lie on the ground, and the dog attacked him repeatedly.

These are not isolated incidents, but rather a clear pattern of escalating violations: violence, medical neglect, and treatment that places him in serious danger.

He had a lot to say. Above all, he wanted to know more about his family and the Palestinian people, and about what is happening on both the Palestinian and Israeli sides. I tried to update him on everything I knew.

But even that conversation took place under absurd conditions: the phones were not working, so we had to shout through the glass just to hear each other.

I sat there for five hours without food or water, trying to make sure that this visit was meaningful.

This is what a legal visit looks like today: deprivation of basic rights, obstruction of communication, and disregard for even the most basic human and professional standards.

And yet, despite all of this, his mind remained sharp, focused, and fully engaged with everything happening beyond the prison walls."

text:
Ancient DNA Sample  Simple Summary
From #Akbari 2026

Published before Disparate demographic impacts of the Roman Colonization and the Migration Period in the Iberian Peninsula, deposited on bioRxiv under the DOI 10.1101/2024.09.23.614606.

This individual was a male buried around 623 AD in a cave burial site in northeastern #Spain (Girona, #Catalonia) during the Visigothic period.

Genetic Background

 Fathers lineage (Y-DNA): J1-L858
This lineage comes from the ancient Near East, especially regions like the Levant and Arabia, and is commonly linked to Semitic populations.

 Mothers lineage (mtDNA): H1
This is a typical Western European lineage, especially common in Iberia, showing local ancestry on the maternal side.

 Overall Ancestry

His genome shows a strong connection to eastern Mediterranean populations, especially groups historically related to:

Jewish communities of North Africa

Southern Europe (Italy, Greece)

His deeper ancestry is made up of:

Early farmers from Anatolia (Turkey region)

Significant Levantine/Natufian ancestry (ancient Near Eastern hunter-gatherers)

Smaller inputs from Iranian farmers, Caucasus groups, and European hunter-gatherers

What This Means

This man was not genetically Visigothic in the northern European sense.

Instead, he represents a mixed Mediterranean profile:

A Near Eastern paternal line

A local Iberian maternal line

A genome similar to eastern Mediterranean / North African Jewish populations

 Likely Historical Scenario

He was probably descended from people who moved west during the Roman Empire, such as:

Soldiers

Traders

Migrants from the eastern Mediterranean

These groups settled in Iberia and mixed with local populations. By the Visigothic period, their descendants were fully integrated culturally, even if their genetic origins were diverse.

 Key Insight

The term Visigothic here reflects culture and political identity, not a uniform genetic background.
This individual is a clear example of how migration and mixing in the Roman world shaped the population of early medieval Spain.

#AncientDNA #Archaeogenetics #VisigothicSpain #RomanEmpire #GeneticHistory #MediterraneanAncestry #NearEasternOrigins #IberianHistory #PopulationGenetics #AncientGenomes #HumanMigration #LevantineAncestry #NorthAfricanJewish #HistoricalGenetics

text:
"Its genuinely heartbreaking that so many who claim Islam today don't even grasp the meaning of Shirk. 

We are talking about the greatest sin imaginable, the sin  will never forgive for whoever dies upon it without repentance, and the crime that condemns a person to eternity in the Fire. 

How have you not found the time to study a matter that decides your eternal fate?

What should be the clearest of all realities has become a subject of debate among people who belong to the very Deen that came to erase Shirk and establish Tawhid. 

If these people actually sat down, opened the Book of , and pondered its verses with the help of the great Mufassirun like Ibn Jarir, Ibn Kathir, al-Qurtubi, and al-Sadi, they would see that the entire Quran is Tawhid.

All of it is a condemnation of Shirk, a warning against its hideous nature, and a clear description of the end of those who practice it.

But instead of learning, they prefer to waste their lives attacking the Dawah of Sheikh Muhammad ibn Abd al-Wahhab, may  have mercy on him. 

May  reward you on behalf of this Ummah, Sheikh, for you called to the pure Tawhid until the ignorant masses in their sheer lack of knowledge began to think you invented these core beliefs. 

They don't realize that your call was nothing more than the call of every Prophet and Messenger from the beginning of time.

I tell you this: Leave the books of the Sheikh aside for a moment, even though they are nothing but Ayat and Ahadith. 

Go directly to the Book of , the Revelation sent to this entire Ummah. Go to the scholars the Ummah has agreed upon for over a thousand years,Ibn Jarir and Ibn Kathir, and actually spend your time studying.

May  guide us and you to the Truth. 

May He protect us from deviation, whims, and the poison of doubts."

text:
Genetic Profile Summary   (Male)

1. Overall Ancestry Profile (Modern Context)

This individual fits squarely within the Western North African (Maghrebi) genetic spectrum, with strong ties to populations from:

Morocco (especially urban/northern regions like Fs)

Tunisia

North African Jewish communities (Libyan, Tunisian, Algerian)

Core Ancestral Components:

North African (Maghrebi / Berber-derived): ~3034%

Levantine (Eastern Mediterranean): ~2030%

Southern European (Mediterranean): ~1317%

Egyptian-related: up to ~18% (model-dependent)

Arabian Peninsula: ~36%

Sub-Saharan African: trace amounts

 Interpretation:

This profile reflects a classic Western Mediterranean fusion zone, where indigenous North African ancestry is layered with:

Eastern Mediterranean gene flow (Levant)

Southern European input (especially island/coastal populations)

Minor trans-Saharan and Arabian influences

2. Deep Ancestry (Prehistoric Breakdown)

Upper Paleolithic (~25,000+ years ago)

Ancient West Eurasian: 96.13%

Ancient African: 3.87%

Closest affinity:

Caucasus-related populations (ancestral to later West Eurasians)

Mesolithic (~10,00015,000 years ago)

A three-way structure typical of the Southern Mediterranean basin:

Natufian (Levant): 36.64%

Anatolian Hunter-Gatherer: 35.34%

Caucasus Hunter-Gatherer: 20.28%

Minor components:

Ancient East African (Mota-like): ~6%

Trace Ancient North Eurasian

Interpretation:

This combination represents the core genetic foundation of Mediterranean and Near Eastern populations, later transmitted into North Africa through migration and diffusion.

3. Paternal Line (Y-DNA): R-Y16006

Lineage Overview:

Belongs to the R1a-Z93 branch, associated with Bronze Age expansions

Emerged approximately 1350 BCE

Migration Context:

Originates from a lineage that moved: 

From Eurasian steppe regions

Into Central Asia  Near East  North Africa

Notable Features:

Rare in North Africa but present through historical migrations

Found today in:

Eastern Europe

Near East

Egypt

Known associations:

Some Jewish Levite lineages

Occasional links to elite or structured male line expansions

 Interpretation:

This paternal line likely entered North Africa through:

Levantine or Jewish-mediated migration routes

Or broader Bronze/Iron Age population movements

4. Maternal Line (mtDNA): M1b2

Lineage Overview:

Deep-rooted lineage dating to ~10,000 BCE

Part of haplogroup M1, which is unusual because: 

It originates outside Africa

Then returns via back-migration into North Africa

Historical Associations:

Strongly linked to: 

Early North African populations

Iberomaurusian cultural horizon

Mediterranean coastal populations

Modern Presence:

North Africa

Southern Europe (Spain, Portugal, Italy)

 Interpretation:

This is a native North African maternal lineage with deep prehistoric continuity and Mediterranean connectivity.

5. Integrated Genetic Interpretation

 represents a textbook Western Mediterranean North African profile, defined by:

Indigenous Maghrebi (Berber-derived) ancestry

Strong Levantine genetic influence

Noticeable Southern European admixture

Minor but real African and Arabian inputs

Lineage Contrast:

Paternal line: intrusive, likely from Bronze Age or later migrations

Maternal line: deeply rooted in North Africa since the early Holocene

 Big Picture:

This individual reflects the historical layering of the Maghreb, shaped by:

Prehistoric Mediterranean populations

Neolithic and Bronze Age expansions

Phoenician, Roman, and later Islamic-era gene flow

Jewish diaspora integration

#AncientDNA #PopulationGenetics #R1aZ93 #M1b2 #HumanOrigins
#GeneticAncestry #WestEurasian #Anthropology #Archaeogenetics #MaghrebHistory
#MediterraneanHistory #Iberomaurusian #LevantineRoots #DNAAnalysis #AncientLineages

text:
" This is Why anaf Scholars Took So Much Caution with Takfr (Obv other Madhabs too)

1 Mull Al al-Qr al-anaf   (d. 1014 AH) said:

The scholars have stated regarding the matter of declaring someone a disbeliever (takfr): if there are 99 possible interpretations indicating kufr (disbelief) and only one that is not kufr, it is better for the muft and the judge to adopt the interpretation that is not kufr.

He further explained that this principle is derived from the saying of the Prophet :

Avert punishments from the Muslims as much as you can. If you find a way out for a Muslim, then let him go.

 [Shar al-Shif al-Q Iy, 2/502]

2 Imm Ibn bidn al-anaf   (d. 1252 AH) said:

The act of disbelief is a grave matter, so I do not declare a believer as a disbeliever. Whenever I find a narration stating [kufr], he is not to be declared as a kfir.

In summary: If there are aspects in an issue that necessitate declaring takfr [excommunication] and one aspect prevents him [from committing kufr], then the muft should lean towards the aspect that prevents declaring takfr on the Muslim, in order to maintain a good opinion towards the Muslim.

Additionally, in al-Bazzyyah, it is added: Except if one explicitly states an intention affirming kufr [disbelief], interpretation [which protected him from takfr] does not benefit them.

 [Radd al-Mutr al al-Durr al-Mukhtr, 4/408]"

text:
"#ISIS #AlQaeda #Iran
After focusing on Syria in its 21 Feb leadership message, urging rival Islamists to join IS due to the Sharaa government's alleged subservience to US, IS has shifted its attention to Al-Qaeda. 

In the latest issue of its weekly paper Al-Naba on 26 Feb, IS claims Al-Qaeda is subservient to Iran, encouraging Al-Qaeda followers/sympathisers to wake up, and presumably switch to IS instead.

IS points to Al-Qaedas 4 Feb message, which called for attacks against US military bases in the Middle East, as evidence of this alleged loyalty to Iran. 
Many anti-Al-Qaeda  jihadist as well as analysts at the time interpreted this call as a sign of Al-Qaedas indirect alignment with Iran, particularly since Al-Qaeda leadership is believed to be based there.

By trying to discredit both Al-Qaeda and the Sharaa government in Syria - the rival global jihadist project and the pragmatic Islamist project, respectively - IS aims to position itself as the only true Muslim group that remains committed to Sharia and that has not allegedly compromised for political gains. 

IS wants to paint itself as the righteous choice for those seeking a genuine Muslim cause"

text:
"Central Bank Official:
Interest System Stopped in All Commercial Banks

Dr. Lutfullah Khairkhwa, Director of Banking and Financial Services at Da Afghanistan Bank, announced that the practice of usury has been eliminated across all commercial banks operating in the country.

According to Khairkhwa, the national banking system is currently undergoing a complete transition away from interest-based transactions toward a fully Islamic banking model.

It may be recalled that Da Afghanistan Bank has previously affirmed that the payment or receipt of interest (riba) is forbidden under Islamic banking principles, and has been pursuing a phased conversion of the countrys conventional banking system to Sharia-compliant banking."

"What is the Nisb of cash money today? How much Zakt do you pay?

Question: How much is a gram of silver today?

Answer: 2.03

Question: What is the Nisb for silver upon which Zakt is paid?

Answer: 595 grams (or above)

Question: What is the Nisb for paper money or money saved in banks?

Answer: Same as the Nisb of silver  i.e., 595 grams (silver).

Question: So, what does that come to today?

Answer: 
It is 595 grams x 2.03 = 1,207.85 GBP
(Which is $1,620.03 USD and 1,394.53 EUR)

If you have this amount or aboveand you've had it for a yearyou must pay Zakt upon it.

Conclusion: If you have this amount of cash [or more] in your possession for a year, you are obligated to pay 2.5% on all of it  as Zakt.

Quick Example:
You have 5,000 in total savings (that you've had for a year): Therefore, you must pay 2.5% of that as your annual Zakt, which comes to 125.

Abu Khadeejah.

Pay online to our UK based registered charity:,

text:
" The bid'ah of the Murji'ah is more harmful than that of the Khawarij. 

The scholars of Ahl al-Sunnah warned strongly about the bid'ah of Irja, to the extent that many of the Salaf considered it more dangerous than the innovation of the Khawarij.

Why?

 The Khawarij reach a limit in misguidance, but the Murji'ah have no boundary; they gradually strip the religion away, piece by piece.

 The Murji'ah open the door to zandaqah (heresy) and disbelief, by emptying iman of action.

 They corrupt the understanding of the religion while appearing outwardly agreeable, even aligning with rulers and worldly interests.

 They widen the door to false interpretations, distorting the texts without restraint.

 They give false security to sinners, claiming salvation despite wrongdoing, resembling the Jews who said:

  "The Fire will not touch us except for a number of days." (al-Baqarah: 80)

Because of this, many of the Salaf (such as al-Nakha'i) said:

"Fitnat al-Murji'ah is more feared upon this Ummah than that of the Azariqah (extreme Khawarij)."

And others said:

"The Khawarij are more excusable to me than the Murji'ah."

The reality is that both groups are harmful, but:

 The Khawarij corrupt through extremism and rebellion.
 The Murjiah corrupt the very foundation of iman itself.

And corruption of the foundation is more severe.

 al-Khorasaniya - Shaykh 'Abd al-Aziz al-Turayfi"

text:
Ar-Rabah ibn Ab Abdir-Ramn said: 

Its not befitting for anyone who knows that he has something from knowledge to ruin himself. 

[Al-Madkhal (no. 687) of al-Bayhaq: a] 

Shaykh Muammad al-Uthaymn said: 

This is a good statement. How [does one ruin himself]? By neglecting the knowledge Allh gave him. Wasting knowledge occurs through neglecting it - not maintaining or caring about it. It also occurs by abandoning acting upon it i.e. that a person maintains it [through revision] but doesnt act upon it; this person is considered to have wasted knowledge, meaning that they didnt benefit from it. 

This should be an admonition for every student of knowledge to maintain what Allah granted them of knowledge. How? By reviewing, revising and acting upon it. For this reason, some [of the scholars] said, Preserve knowledge by acting upon it, while others said, Preserve it by writing it down, and both are correct [as one should do both]. 

(Shar a al-Bukhr)

text:
"#FreeAafiaNow #BringAafiaHome
The US war on Muslim women.
The Story of Aafia Siddiqui . A Mother, A Sister, A Prisoner. The most oppressed woman in the world.

She was kidnapped. Sold. Raped. Her children were torn from her arms and sold alongside her, and what followed is one of the most harrowing stories of injustice in modern history.
Aafia Siddiqui, a Pakistani neuroscientist and mother of three, was abducted along with her young children. The Pakistani government sold her and her babies to the CIA for $55,000, a transaction that treated a mother and her children as commodities.

What was done to those children defies imagination.

Her infant son, Sulaiman, was just six months old when a CIA agent killed him, slamming his head to the ground in front of his six-year-old brother, Ahmed. Ahmed was then taken to Afghanistan, tortured, and imprisoned for five years.

Her daughter, Maryam, was only three years old. She was forcibly placed with a Christian family who changed her name and kept her for seven years.

As for Aafia herself, she was tortured, raped, and is currently serving an 86-year sentence in a US federal prison. Never once charged with terrorism. Never convicted of a violent crime.

This is United States terrorism on Muslim women. A mother, a daughter, a sister, targeted, brutalised, and discarded. Her only crime was her faith and her identity.

And what is perhaps most shocking is the silence.

The silence of human rights organizations. The silence of NGOs. The silence of the international community, the very institutions that exist to speak for the voiceless, that march and campaign and raise millions in the name of justice. Where are they for Aafia? Where are they for her children?

Their silence is not neutrality. Their silence is complicity.

This is what was done to our sister. May she never be forgotten."

text:
Shaykh al-Allamah Abd al-Azz l Ash-Shaykh     said:

Shaykh Abd al-Azz al-Rji is one of our virtuous brothers, one of the scholars, and one of the God-fearing people (al-atqiy) who is known for worship and the recitation of the Qurn. He is recognized for his knowledge, purity of tongue, and chastity of speech. He is among the students of Shaykh Abd al-Azz bin Bz (may Allah have mercy upon him), had a strong connection to him, and was influenced by the Shaykhs character.

The person is well-known to everyone for the ilm nfi (beneficial knowledge) and steadfastness he possesses, his memorization of the Book of Allah, his constant recitation of the Qurn, his night prayers (qiym al-layl), his fasting of voluntary days, and the knowledge he has disseminated. May Allah grant him success and grant him a goodly enjoyment; he is among the best of the Muslim scholars. We ask Allah for thabt (steadfastness) for us, for you, and for him.

[From an audio clip]

text:
"'    

The Ashari view of Gods actions is rooted in a strict form of divine command theory (DCT), where no logically possible act is deemed unbefitting of Him. This approach raises deeper questions about the nature of truth and divine guidance. 

For example, on the Ash'ari view, Does God have to tell the truth? This question gets to the core of a deep problem that underlies their strict DCT view. 

Ill break it down in simple terms here. But first, a disclaimer:

                .               .

 B:

- Asharis believe that no logically possible act is unbefitting of God. Any action you can possibly conceive of is an equally possible and valid expression of His will as any other. On this view, there is nothing about God that makes the creation of this world any more likely than a world with nothing but a pile of dog feces created for absolutely no reason (excuse the stark example, but it helps illustrate the point clearly). He chose to create this world instead of the feces world for literally no reason at all.

- For example, on this view, a world that contains nothing but an innocent baby suffering in Hell for an eternity for absolutely no reason is perfectly befitting of God and His perfection. It is axiologically identical to a world where the righteous are rewarded and only the sinful are punished. Both possible realities are equally befitting of God, and theres absolutely no reason or wisdom that can explain why He chose to do one instead of the other.

- Even in terms of our deeds: God doesnt prefer one deed over another, unless by prefer you mean He just arbitrarily willed for it to happen (for no reason). The most pious worshipper to have ever lived is identical to Iblis in terms of Gods preference or love. Unless we understand love as Gods will to reward the worshipper, which again, is completely arbitrary and could have gone in the exact opposite direction with the worshiper getting punished and Iblis getting rewarded. Both equally possible and equally befitting of God. The only reason we assume that one is better than the other is because thats what weve become accustomed to.

So, on the Ashari view, Gods actions are completely arbitrary. He doesnt act for any specific reason. He creates clouds through which He creates rain, but He didnt create the clouds for that reason. It just happens to be the case that he created clouds followed by rain.

  

So, where does the truthfulness question come in?

Well, if all logically possible acts are equally befitting of God, and theres nothing about Him that explains why He chooses to act one way instead of another except for brute arbitrary will, then how can you be sure He tells the truth?

They cant appeal to perfection, as Al-Juwayni and others explain, because then the entire foundation collapses and we could (as we do) similarly appeal to perfection when explaining why any of the above possibilities is unbefitting of God.

The typical Ashari move here is to argue that its logically impossible for God to lie. They say that Gods created speech (like the Arabic Qur'an) necessarily indicates His internal speech.

Therefore, lying is impossible because created speech cannot conflict with the internal meaning it indicates.

But even if thats true, it doesnt solve the real issue, namely: How do you know  that this SPECIFIC instance of "created" speechlike the Qur'ancorresponds to Gods internal speech?

  " "

In response, Asharis would typically appeal to miracles. 

The idea is that when you see a prophet perform miracles, God creates an unshakable certainty in your mind that the prophet is truthful.

The logic is that miracles are like a seal of approval from God proving that the prophet speaks the truth.

They use the common analogy of the king to explain this:

Imagine a king in a grand gathering. A man stands up and says, Im the kings messenger and will speak on behalf of him. To prove it, the man turns to the king and says, O King, if Im truthful, stand up and sit back down as a sign. When the king, who doesnt usually do such a thing, actually complies, everyone in the room instantly knows the man is truthful.

Asharis argue that this analogous to what happens when God displays miracles through a prophet. It creates undeniable certainty that the prophet is truthful.

And of course, this is true in general, but not on Ashari grounds!

Why? Because they also believe that God acts for no reasons whatsoever and that any logically conceivable act is in line with His perfection.

This analogy only works if the king acts with purpose; if he has "reasons" behind his actions. If the kings actions are just randomif he stands up and sits down for no reason whatsoeverthen the act doesnt prove anything.

He couldn't have done it with the intention of demonstrating the mans truthfulness because he never does one thing with the intention of achieving another in the first place.

So, God has the ability to create miracles at the hands if a false prophet, and on the Ashari view, whether He would choose to do so or not is entirely arbitrary: no one act is more preferable or likely than the other with respect to His nature and perfection.

And as Ibn Taymiyya says, appealing to "necessity" to justify knowing one thing over another, when theres no objective difference between the two whatsoever, ultimately destroys any reliable basis for knowledge.

 " " 

Finally, one of the most common responses they present is that if false prophets were allowed to perform miracles, it would undermine Gods ability to reveal truth to humanity.

They reason that if false prophets could perform miracles, it would blur the line between truth and falsehood, making it impossible for God to guide people to the truth through miracles.

So they argue that this would compromise Gods omnipotence, as it would entail that He is unable to demonstrate the truthfulness of a prophet, and therefore, it could never happen.

However, this argument falls apart when examined against their broader theological framework. Heres why:

1.       

The first issue is that the Ashari view itself makes the very act of demonstrating a prophets truthfulness through miracles impossible. Their framework denies that God acts with purpose or intention, which means there is no reason behind any action He performs. But for miracles to serve as evidence of prophethood, there must be a purposeful link between the miracle and the claim it validates.

For example, if a king sends a messenger with a unique seal to prove his authority, the seal serves as evidence only because the king intentionally assigned it that role. Without intention, the seal is meaningless.

Similarly, miracles cannot inherently signify truthfulness unless God intentionally creates them for that purpose.

Since the Ashari view denies that God acts with reasons, it follows that demonstrating truthfulness through miracles is inherently impossible in their framework. And if an act is inherently impossible, divine power does not "attach" to itjust as it does not "attach" to creating a square circle.

This makes their argument self-destruct. If their framework already excludes the possibility of demonstrating truth through miracles, then allowing miracles for false prophets would not compromise Gods power, as it cannot attach to something impossible in the first place.

2.    () 

When confronted with the problem of intentionality, some Asharis respond by appealing to talazumthe idea that one thing can logically necessitate another. 

They argue that miracles could inherently serve as evidence of truthfulness through a built-in necessary connection, without requiring any purposeful act from God. For example, they might say that just as knowledge necessitates life, a miracle could necessitate the truthfulness of a prophets claim.

But this argument fails because miracles do not intrinsically signify truthfulness. Their role as evidence of genuine prophethood relies on being intentionally assigned that role by God. Without such purpose or reason, a miracle could just as easily be a random act of power, with no connection to the truth of a prophets claim.

Think of it like a flag on a battlefield. A flag might signify victory, but only if it is intentionally raised for that purpose. If the flag is raised randomly or blown into the air by the wind, it no longer serves as a meaningful indicator of victory.

Similarly, for miracles to signify the truthfulness of a prophet, there must be a purposeful act linking the miracle to its role as evidence.

Without any such reasons behind God's actions, there is no necessary connection between miracles and truthfulness. Their appeal to talazum collapses because the connection between a miracle and its meaning is not intrinsic; it must be purposefully assigned.

3.       

This "compromised power" response also leads to a deeper dilemma: is it possible for God to allow a false prophet to perform miracles, or not?

They are left with two options, both of which are problematic:

)            :

This position is correct, because it aligns with the idea that Gods wisdom and perfection would never permit the validation of falsehood. However, it also contradicts their framework, which denies that any act can be inherently unbefitting or inappropriate for God.

For them, all logically possible acts are equally befitting of God's perfection, so they cannot consistently claim that God would never allow miracles at the hands of a false prophet without such an act being impossible in itself.

)           :

This position compromises Gods omnipotence by making something possible in itself (creating miracles at the hands of a false prophet) impossible for God. It implies a deficiency in divine power.
----------------------------------

In short, the Ashari stance on the arbitrariness of Gods actions, along with their belief that no possible act is "unbefitting" of Him, leads to serious foundational issues with the concepts of truth and divine guidance."

text:
"Salafis do not believe that the pagans of Makkah possessed a complete understanding of Tawheed al-Rububiyyah. When they assert that the pagans affirmed Tawheed al-Rububiyyah, they are referring to its foundational aspect (asl), which is the belief in one God as the ultimate creator and sustainer of the universe, the only being who is entirely independent. Salafis emphasize this point in response to saint venerators who argue that they are not guilty of shirk simply because they do not consider their saints to be independent of Allah. Salafis contend that the independence of a being is not the sole criterion for determining whether certain beliefs or actions constitute major shirk.

Salafis are well aware that the pagan Quraysh, while acknowledging Allah as the supreme creator, also attributed daughters to Him, worshipped idols, and failed to affirm His attributes correctly. These theological errors are considered distortions of Tawheed al-Rububiyyah.

Prominent Salafi scholars, such as Shaykh Abdulrahman Al-Barrak and Shaykh Yusuf Al-Ghafees, have argued that saint venerators also commit shirk in Rububiyyah by ascribing certain powers to their saints, powers that should be attributed exclusively to Allah.

To avoid semantic confusion, if Rububiyyah is primarily understood as Allahs independence and self-sufficiency, then Salafis assert that most of the pagan Quraysh acknowledged this foundational aspect. (However, Salafi scholars such as Shaykh Sultan al-Umayri, Abdulrazzaq Abu Badr, and Abu Bakr Muhammad Zakariyyah note that the Quraysh were not a homogeneous group and differed in their beliefs.) If Rububiyyah is defined in broader terms, while still rooted in the foundation of Allahs independence, Salafis maintain that the pagans only affirmed this foundational aspect.

The central issue, according to Salafis, is the affirmation of Allahs independence and the rejection of any justification by saint venerators that their actions do not constitute major shirk."

"it is not permissible for a person to become a legal consultant in man-made laws, or to study in such laws in institutions that teach them, or to enter colleges that teach man-made laws and disbelief in Allah, may Allah protect us and you from that, as exists in some countries.

Whoever learns the Shariah and becomes knowledgeable may refute the falsehood of the Kuffar. But for a person to say he is studying what the Kuffar brought and similar matters, this is misguidance, may Allah protect us and you.

There is nothing in Islam called a college of law in this sense; rather, there are Shariah colleges that teach the Book and the Sunnah. These are the legitimate institutions that teach the law of Allah.

Shaykh Abdullah as Sa'd (may Allah free him) -Explanation of Sunan al-Tirmidhi, Kitab al-Salah,Lesson 73",

text:
""A short afternoon nap restores brain neuroplasticity. 
"
--------------

The Prophet  said:

Take the midday rest (qayllah), for the devils do not take a midday rest.

And from the Salaf, Khawwt ibn Jubayr    said:

Sleeping at the beginning of the day is harmful, in the middle of it is restorative, and at its end is foolishness.



Once again modern science is catching up to what the Prophet  guided us to over 1400 years ago. 

As Muslims we need to understand that the Sunnah isnt just spiritual guidance that happens to agree with science sometimes; it is advice from our Creator who knows best how our bodies and brains function optimally.

This is exactly why studying the Prophets  guidance on health is so important; its something to be learned and actually implemented in daily life. If we had the same level of reliance upon that guidance as we do on modern medicine, we wouldnt need to look elsewhere.

text:
7. Buying and selling firecrackers: Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (What I see is that buying and selling them is Haram (prohibited), and that is for two reasons. The first reason: it is a waste of money, and wasting money is prohibited due to the Prophet's, peace and blessings be upon him, forbidding of that. The second: it causes harm to people with its disturbing noises, and it may cause fires if it falls on something flammable while it is alive [lit] and not extinguished.)  Majmoo Al-Fatawa (3/3).
> 
8. Al-Qaza': Which is shaving part of the head and leaving part of it: From Ibn Umar, may Allah be pleased with them both: (That the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, forbade Al-Qaza'.)  Narrated by Al-Bukhari (5921), and Muslim (2120).
And from him, may Allah be pleased with him, he said: (The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings be upon him, saw a boy who had part of his hair shaved and part of it left, so he forbade them from that and said: 'Shave it all, or leave it all.')  Narrated by Abu Dawud (4195).
It is said in Al-Qamoos:
> (Al-Qaza' is to shave the head of a boy and leave separate spots un-shaved, resembling the Qaza' (scattered patches) of clouds, and similarly in An-Nihayah.)  Ghidha' Al-Albab (1/429).
> 
Sheikh Abdul Mohsen Al-Abbad, may Allah preserve him, said in his explanation of Sunan Abi Dawud:
> (And included in Al-Qaza' is what some youth do in barbershops by shaving the hair at the nape of the neck seeking adornment; because shaving part of the head and leaving part of it leads to the same result, whether it is intended for adornment or not intended for adornment, except if the shaving is for medical treatment or for Hijamah (cupping), then there is no harm in this.)
> 
9. Girls wearing short clothing:
Sheikh Ibn Uthaymeen, may Allah have mercy on him, said:
> (I see that a person should not dress his daughter in this short clothing while she is young, because if she gets used to it, she will remain upon it and its matter will become trivial to her. As for if she is accustomed to modesty from her youth, she will remain in that state in her adulthood. And what I advise our Muslim sisters is to abandon the clothing of the people abroad from the enemies of the religion, and to accustom their daughters to covering clothing, and to modesty, for modesty is from faith.)  Majmoo Fatawa wa Rasa'il (12/274).
> 
10. Not listening to the Eid sermon: Some people, after praying the Eid prayer, leave and do not listen to the sermon, and this is a mistake. Imam Malik was asked about a man who prays with the Imam on the day of Al-Fitr, is it permissible for him to leave before hearing the sermon? He said: (He should not leave until the Imam leaves.)  Ahkam Al-'Idayn by Al-Faryabi (144).
 There are many violations that occur during Eid; we have mentioned some of them. I ask Allah to benefit me and you through them, and Allah knows best.

And let him not accept any statement from anyone unless he demands from him, in proof of its correctness, either:

1) a clear, decisive Ayah, or 
2) an established Sunnah, or 
3) the statement of a Sahab with a sound chain.

text:
A Concise Summary on the Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid 

Sheikh Saleem Al-Hilli afidhahullh states:

All praise is due to Allh, Lord of the Worlds, and peace and blessings be upon the Master of the Messengers, Muammad, and upon his family, companions, and those who follow them with excellence until the Day of Judgment.

My dear brothers and sisters...There has been much discussion regarding this matter (i.e. Concurrence of Jumuah and Eid). Some have asked: Does one prayer suffice for the other, or is it obligatory for a Muslim to perform both the Eid and Jumuah prayers?

Many who have spoken on this issue have done so without the requisite qualification to understand the evidences transmitted from the Messenger of Allh . This is because they approach the matter based on a flawed methodology: they first adopt a belief, then seek out supporting evidence. This is a mistake! The correct methodology is to derive beliefs from evidence, not to impose ones beliefs or schools of thought upon the evidence.

Therefore, I shall briefly present the adths and reports attributed to the Messenger of Allh  and the noble Companionsmay Allh be pleased with themthen explain the positions of the recognised schools of Fiqh on this issue, relying upon Allh Almighty.

So I say, with Allhs guidance, that there are both prophetic traditions (marf) and reports from the companion (mawqf) on this matter.

The first is the adth of Zayd ibn Arqam (may Allh be pleased with him), in which Muwiyah ibn Ab Sufyn (may Allh be pleased with him) asked him: Did you witness the Messenger of Allh  having two Eids occur on the same day? He replied, Yes. He asked, What did he do? Zayd said, He performed the Eid prayer, then granted a concession regarding the Jumuah prayer, saying: Whoever wishes to pray, let him pray. This adth was narrated by Amad, Ab Dwd, An-Nas, Ibn Mjah, Ad-Drim, and Al-Hkim, and it is authentic.

The second adth is from Ab Hurayrah (may Allh be pleased with him), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we will indeed perform the Jumuah. This adth was also narrated by Ab Dwd and Ibn Mjah, and it is authentic.

The third adth is from Ibn Umar (may Allh be pleased with him), who said: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh . He led the people in prayer, then said: Whoever wishes to attend the Jumuah may do so, and whoever wishes to refrain may do so. This adth was narrated by Ibn Mjah. A-abarn also transmitted it with the wording: Two Eids coincided during the time of the Messenger of Allh  on the day of Eid Al-Fir and Friday. The Messenger of Allh  led them in the Eid prayer, then turned to face them and said: O people, you have achieved good and reward. Indeed, we are holding the Jumuah. Whoever wishes to join us may do so, and whoever wishes to return to his family may do so. This narration is also authentic.

The fourth adth is from Ibn Abbs (may Allh be pleased with them both), in which the Messenger of Allh  said: Two Eids have coincided on this day of yours. Whoever wishes, it will suffice him in place of Jumuah. But we are gathering, in-sh-Allh. This adth is also authentic and narrated by Ibn Mjah.

From the reports of the Companions: A ibn Ab Rab said: Ibn Az-Zubayr led us in the Eid prayer on a Friday early in the day. Then we proceeded to attend Jumuah, but he did not come out to us, so we prayed individually. Ibn Abbs was in A-if, and when he returned and we mentioned this to him, he said: He acted in accordance with the Sunnah. This was reported by Ab Dwd, and by Ibn Khuzaymah with a different wording and with an addition: Ibn Az-Zubayr said: I saw Umar ibn Al-Khab, when two Eids coincided, do the same.

""Bir ifti, satmak iin bir eek ve bir keiyi ehre gtryordu. Keinin boynunda bir ngrak vard.

 hrsz, iftinin getiini grd. 

Birincisi dedi ki: Keiyi alacam, hem de ifti fark etmeden.

kincisi dedi ki: Ben de eeini alrm.

ncs ise dedi ki:  Ben de onu tamamen soyacam.

Birinci hrsz gizlice keiye yaklat, ngra karp eein kuyruuna balad ve keiyi alp bir tarlaya gtrd.

Yolun ortasnda ifti arkasna bakt ve keinin kaybolduunu fark etti. Onu aramaya koyuldu.

kinci hrsz yanna gelip ne aradn sordu. ifti keisinin alndn syledi. 

Hrsz dedi ki: Az nce burada, u ormanda bir adamn keiyle kotuunu grdm. Yetimen kolay olur.

ifti, eeini ona emanet edip keisinin peine dt. Hrsz ise eei alp gitti.

ifti ormandan geri dndnde eeini de bulamad. Alamaya balad ve yoluna devam etti. Yolda, bir batakln kenarnda alayan bir adam grd. Ona ne olduunu sordu. 

Adam dedi ki: ehre altn dolu bir uval gtryordum. Dinlenmek iin burada oturdum. Uyuyunca uvala arptm, suya dt.

ifti, Neden karp almyorsun? dedi.

Adam: Sudan korkuyorum, yzme bilmiyorum. Ama karana yirmi altn veririm. dedi.

ifti sevindi: Allah bunu bana, keim ve eeimin kaybn telafi etmek iin verdi. dedi.

Elbiselerini karp suya indi. Ama altn dolu uval bulamad. Sudan ktnda ise elbiselerini de bulamad.

Bu da nc hrszn iiydi. Oda, iftinin elbiselerini almay baarmt."

Bu hikye, insanlarn en ok zarar grd  durumu anlatr:

1-  Gaflet (Habersiz) annda arkadan gelen haner (darbe).

2-  Nasihat eder gibi grnp zehirleyen kiidir.

3- Duygulara oynayp insan kandran (aldatan) kiidir."

text:
"Update on Sheikh Ali Al-Timimi:

Five years ago, Dr. Ali was released into home confinement, where he remains. At that time, he had served 15 years of a life plus 70-year sentence, all of which was in solitary confinement and under SAMS. 

In the summer of 2024, the lower court finally ruled in his favour and removed the life and 30 years from his sentence, leaving approximately 25 years of the original sentence. Since then, Dr. Ali has finally been allowed to appeal the original conviction and the remaining 25 years of that sentence. 

This is the first time in US legal history that a person has had to wait 20 years to have the opportunity to appeal his convictions.

Depending on how the appellate court rules, Dr. Ali will be cleared of the charges against him or compelled to return to prison to complete the remaining 25 years.

Given that he is in his 60s, a 25-year sentence is an effective death sentence. Please keep him in your prayers and inform as many well-wishers as possible.

 Idris Palmer (@AIdrisPalmer)"

text:
"Wallahi an unforgettable journey! From Kuwait to Makkah, it was truly beautiful! and that is the same sentiment anyone who attended has. The many sittings, whether with the people of knowledge or the students of knowledge or just the many brotherly sittings over good food or coffee, the whole occasion was monumental and hugely beneficial for the soul!

It brings to mind the statement of Imm Ibnil Qayyim:

Keeping the company of the righteous transforms you from six states to six others:
1.From doubt to certainty.
2.From showing off (riy) to sincerity.
3.From heedlessness to remembrance (dhikr).
4.From desiring the worldly life to desiring the Hereafter.
5.From arrogance to humility.
6.From bad intention to genuine sincere naah.

(Ighthat al-Lahfn 1/136)

May Allah return us for many more!",

text:
A tremendous evening with our brothers and sisters in London at @_MuSunnah in Alperton (Thursday 9th April 2026). 

Msh-Allh, the Markaz was filled with listeners. With barely a days notice, Ahlus-Sunnah travelled from all across London and beyond to join us. The evening began with a dars by Shaikh @uwaysT followed by Shaikh @AbuIdrees and finally a reminder from myself. 

It is important that we learn and convey this Deen with sincerity and good manners, becoming good examples for those around usthat we are humble and distant from the corrupting influences of social mediathat we take the Messenger Muhammad (peace and blessings be upon him) as our example, and then his Companions (Allah be pleased with them)that we love those those who love and follow them, for they are Ahlul-Hadth was-Sunnahand thereafter, the foremost among them in every age are the scholars (may Allah raise their rank). I can say that over the years, we have benefited greatly from the noble manners of the ulamshaping the way we behave and deal with the peopleand I seek pardon from Allah for my shortcomings.

So, in the West, our communities should focus on seeking knowledge; then act on what we learn, cultivate our children, nurture righteousness in our homes and hold fast to the Sunnah. 

Furthermore, Hijrah should be an important discussion in our households: actively planning and searching for a home in a Muslim country where there are already Salafi communities, however small. 

Know that Hijrah from non-Muslim lands to the lands of Islam is an obligationand a protection for you, your family and children; a safety net against the abandonment of religion today and in generations to come, insh-Allh. 

I also advise that you should not waste your time (and wealth) following the fashions, sinful habits and clothing of the non-Muslims because Islam has its own unique and proud heritage and identitynor should you follow and obsess over the modern-day offerings of entertainment (such as football, boxing and movies) that are designed to divert people away from the realities of life and its purpose: the worship of Allah and following the Messenger (peace and blessings be upon him). Focus, O Muslim, on what benefits you in this life and the Hereafter, brakallhu feekum. 

Do not be impressed by social media influencers who seek only views, likes and followers. Our example is the Prophet of Islamwho taught us to be humble, truthful and sincerethen his Companions and the Righteous Salaf. In these times you see that example reflected in the manners of the scholars and their conduct (may Allah preserve the living and have mercy upon the deceased). 

So, I advise you to get off your mobile devices and drastically limit viewing social media platformsinstead read books, attend duroos, get married, have children, focus on your families and build true Islamic homes. 

May Allah aid the Muslims and guide them to the Sunnah and the path of Ahlul-Hadthand that is the Madhhab of the Salaf. 

In thisby Allahthere is honour, just as Umar (Allah be pleased with him) said: We were a lowly people, and Allah honoured us with Islam. So if we seek honour in other than Islam, then Allah will make us lowly.

Abu Khadeejah.
